title: Add DigitalOcean firewall lifecycle for managed boxes
description: Add DigitalOcean cloud-firewall lifecycle support for managed boxes. The desired shape is a temporary bootstrap SSH aperture scoped to operator/admin IPs when needed, UDP 41641 for Tailscale, no public app ports by default, and removal of public 22/tcp after Tailscale enrollment. Model route and authority: Codex gpt-5.5 owns orchestration, system design, implementation, review, commit acceptance, and final say. Claude Opus is only for UX/docs visual review if a docs surface needs design judgment. Grok/Composer 2.5 may run bounded probes only when the validation commands and stronger-model review are explicit. Stop condition: never apply real firewall mutations without dry-run output and mocked tests first.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: box up dry-run shows intended DO firewall create/update/attach/lockdown actions; implementation is idempotent; status can report the firewall id/name/rules attached to the droplet; failures surface structured recovery hints. Validation: mocked doctl tests cover create, attach, update, already-present, and failure paths; no live cloud mutation is required for unit tests.
notes: No-ragrets contract. Outcome: DigitalOcean managed boxes get an idempotent cloud firewall lifecycle with a short bootstrap aperture and lockdown after Tailscale. Evidence: dry-run actions, mocked doctl tests, attached firewall identity/rules in status, and recovery hints. Failure avoided: future boxes rely on manually configured cloud firewalls that agents cannot verify or reproduce.
close_reason: Added DO firewall CRUD (do_create_firewall, do_update_firewall_lockdown, do_delete_firewall, do_get_firewall), bootstrap creates temporary SSH aperture firewall, post-enrollment locks down to Tailscale UDP only, cmd_down cleans up firewall. 2 new tests.

title: Resolve critical disk pressure before broad validation
description:
Reality check: the broad unittest lane hit `No space left on device` once, and local pressure remained critical after partial temporary cleanup. Broad validation cannot be trusted while the machine may fail tests or generated artifacts from ENOSPC.

Fix direction: start with read-only pressure inventory, protect declared runtime/secret state, then either recover enough local free space for the broad suite or route broad validation through an approved offloaded path.
design:
writes:
  - none
stop_rules:
  - If free space is insufficient and no offload path is already approved, record the blocker instead of deleting data.
non_goals:
  - Do not perform Docker prune, cache deletion, or ballast changes from this node.
global_constraints:
  - Read-only by default; no deletion/truncation/moves without explicit operator approval.
  - Never touch protected agent history or secrets: ~/.codex, ~/.claude, .env, .env.box, workspace/secrets, client overlays.
  - Do not commit; integration lead owns final commit decision.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
* pressure-report and/or disk-space-triage inventory is recorded.
* Protected paths are not deleted, truncated, or moved.
* There is enough local free space for broad validation, or an approved offload/skip blocker is recorded.
* No cleanup is performed without explicit approval where deletion is involved.
notes: Blocked: pressure-report remains critical (13.22 GiB free, 2.87% free; df shows 13Gi available at 97% capacity). No cleanup approval and no approved offload path; broad unittest lane intentionally not run under stop rule. Result artifact: <skillbox-config>/clients/skillbox/invocations/build000r/skillbox/divide-and-conquer/2026-05-19T00-38-10Z/WG-004_RESULT.md
close_reason: Resolved by environment recovery, no cleanup performed. pressure-report 2026-05-29: local_disk.free_gib=71.5 (free_percent 15.53), pressure_level=elevated, min_free_gib=10.0 — well above the 10 GiB stop threshold that gated 2026-05-19 (was 13.22 GiB / 2.87%). Broad validation proven safe: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests ran 1061 tests OK (skipped=1) in 181s with no ENOSPC. Protected paths (~/.codex, ~/.claude, .env, .env.box, overlays) untouched; no deletion/truncation/moves.

comments.author: b
comments.text: Current-run update: broad validation was started after focused gates passed, then stopped before completion at operator direction because free disk dropped under 10 GiB during python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. No broad pass result exists for this run; disk pressure remains the blocker. Do not rerun broad validation locally until space is recovered or an offload path is approved.

title: Docs & onboarding: architecture doc, thin README, license, posture truth, demo
description:
EPIC: Restructure documentation for the project's actual audiences (cold operators, cold agents, future maintainers): a standalone ARCHITECTURE.md, a thin README backed by docs/ pages, a license decision, honest network-posture docs (MODES D1), and a runnable onboarding demo.

CURRENT STATE (2026-06-12 docs audit): README.md is 2,231 lines carrying architecture, command reference, runtime-graph internals, troubleshooting, and FAQ in one scroll; docs/ has strong operational guides (VISION, ROADMAP, tailnet lifecycle/ingress, shared-jam) but no architecture doc, no API reference (covered by the registry-docs bead in the ergonomics epic), and NO LICENSE file (README admits it). MODES D1 found posture docs not matching the implemented default.

PRINCIPLE: generated beats hand-written wherever a source of truth exists (registry -> API reference; this epic only hand-writes what has no machine source). Nothing is deleted — content MOVES with link redirects.

EXIT: a new operator reaches a working box from README in one sitting without scrolling past internals; a maintainer finds the system map in ARCHITECTURE.md; the legal status of the repo is explicit.

title: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: layers, manifests, modules, data flow, state machine
description:
OBJECTIVE: Write docs/ARCHITECTURE.md — the maintainer-grade system map: layers, module inventory, data flow, state layout, and the box lifecycle state machine — extracted from the README's embedded section and the 2026-06-12 architecture audits.

BACKGROUND: the only architecture description lives inside README.md (~lines 1873-1939, a single diagram + prose); the real map — 43 runtime_manager modules totaling ~37.5k lines, the brain's graph->algorithms->engine->decisions pipeline, the outer scripts layer (box.py 4.2k lines, reconcile, operator MCP), four YAML manifests with distinct roles, and the .skillbox-state layout — exists only in audit transcripts and code. Every deep change (this plan included) re-derives it expensively.

REASONING: a 60k-line system maintained by agents NEEDS its map as a durable artifact; agents load ARCHITECTURE.md once instead of re-exploring per session — the single highest-leverage doc for agent-intuitiveness.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: Sections: (1) Layer diagram (mermaid): operator machine (box.py, operator MCP, boxes.json) / host (docker, tailscale, scripts 01-07) / container (workspace, agent homes) / runtime manager (manage.py, pulse, in-box MCP) / brain. (2) Manifest roles table: sandbox.yaml=container shape, dependencies.yaml=surface categories, runtime.yaml=operational graph, persistence.yaml=state bindings, skill-repos.yaml=skill supply chain — one paragraph each with file paths. (3) Module inventory: per runtime_manager module, responsibility + key entry points (source from the 2026-06-12 audit; verify line counts at writing time, they drift). (4) Data flow (mermaid): runtime.yaml -> build_runtime_model -> filter -> {lifecycle ops, agent graph -> algorithms -> decisions/next} with adapter evidence joins. (5) Box lifecycle state machine (mermaid stateDiagram from VALID_TRANSITIONS — keep next to a pointer at box.py so edits prompt doc updates). (6) State layout: .skillbox-state tree with owner/writer per path. (7) Extension recipes: "add a command" (registry+cli+mcp+test checklist), "add a service", "add an adapter", "add a graph algorithm" — each 5-10 lines pointing at the real seams. README's architecture section shrinks to a pointer (full move happens in the README-restructure bead; this bead may land the pointer early). Link from AGENTS.md Project Shape.

FILES: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (new), AGENTS.md (link), README.md (pointer only).

RISKS: staleness-by-design — mitigate by sourcing numbers sparingly (responsibilities age well, line counts do not; include counts in one clearly-dated table) and by the extension recipes referencing test-enforced seams (registry contract tests keep those honest).

ACCEPTANCE: (1) all seven sections present; (2) mermaid diagrams render on GitHub; (3) every module in runtime_manager/ appears in the inventory; (4) extension recipes verified by following one (add-a-command dry walk) during review; (5) linked from AGENTS.md and README.

TESTING: link-validity covered by the README-restructure bead's link checker (or a minimal relative-link test added here if this lands first); mermaid syntax validated via the repo's mmdx tooling.

OBSERVABILITY: n/a.

MAINTAINER NOTES: This doc's contract: WHERE things live and WHY the seams exist — never duplicate WHAT (that is the API reference's job, and it is generated). When they disagree, the generated doc wins and this one gets fixed.
close_reason: Added docs/ARCHITECTURE.md system map with links from README and AGENTS

title: README restructure: <500-line front door + focused docs/ pages + link checker
description:
OBJECTIVE: Restructure the 2,231-line README into a <500-line front door plus focused docs/ pages — preserving every word of content as MOVED, not deleted — with a link checker that keeps the web of references valid.

BACKGROUND: README.md currently serves five audiences at once: pitch (TL;DR, comparisons, vision), operator quickstart, complete command reference, subsystem internals (runtime graph, skill sync, client projection, distribution, parity ledger, local profiles, swimmers, forge, worker broker, pressure tools), troubleshooting, and FAQ. The docs audit measured the cost: the install path is buried mid-scroll; agents grep it like a database. AGENTS.md + the search brain index README — structure changes must keep those consumers working.

REASONING: each audience gets a page sized to its need; the README's job narrows to orient-and-route in one screen-page per concern. The registry-generated API reference (ergonomics epic) and ARCHITECTURE.md (sibling bead) absorb the two heaviest sections, which is why both block this bead.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: (1) Target README: badges/pitch, 10-line quickstart, the why/when-not-to-use tables (they are the marketing surface — keep), command-surface OVERVIEW table linking to docs/API_REFERENCE.md, doc map, contribution/license pointers. (2) New/expanded pages: docs/runtime-graph.md (runtime.yaml, overlays, profiles, tasks, services, parity ledger, local-* profiles, error-code tables), docs/clients.md (client-init/open/project/publish/diff, blueprints, private repo split, first-box), docs/skills.md (skill-repos, lockfiles, visibility layers, forge, distribution), docs/operations.md (focus, pulse, sessions, worker broker, swimmers, pressure/RCH/SBH), docs/troubleshooting.md, docs/faq.md. Move verbatim-first, edit-after — the PR shows moves, a follow-up commit may tighten prose. (3) Anchors: README sections that move leave a one-line pointer for inbound links (external links target README anchors today). (4) Link checker: tests/test_doc_links.py — walk *.md in repo root + docs/, resolve relative links and intra-doc anchors, fail on dangling (stdlib html.parser/re; no new deps). (5) Update consumers: AGENTS.md references, agent_search doc-index list (it indexes README.md/AGENTS.md — add docs/ pages to the index in the same PR so brain.search keeps finding moved content; that is a small agent_search.py change with a test).

FILES: README.md, docs/*.md (new pages), AGENTS.md, runtime_manager/agent_search.py (+test), tests/test_doc_links.py (new).

RISKS: content loss during the move (mitigate: word-count + heading-census before/after recorded in the PR; reviewer diffs the census, not 2k lines); breaking the context-drift goldens if any golden embeds README text (audit first).

ACCEPTANCE: (1) README <500 lines; (2) heading census shows every pre-move section present somewhere; (3) link checker green and wired into CI; (4) brain.search finds content moved to docs/ (test with two known phrases); (5) quickstart reachable without scrolling past internals (first 100 lines).

TESTING: link checker; search-index test; census procedure in PR.

OBSERVABILITY: n/a.

MAINTAINER NOTES: The census discipline exists because "we lost the parity-ledger error table in a docs shuffle" is exactly the kind of regression nobody notices for months. Move, verify, then polish.

title: DECISION: repository license / legal posture (operator input required)
description:
DECISION: Choose and publish the repository's license / legal posture. OPERATOR INPUT REQUIRED — agents must not select a license unilaterally.

BACKGROUND: README Limitations states "No license file is included yet"; the repo is public-ish ("opensource/skillbox" path, public install.sh curl instructions targeting github.com/build000r/skillbox) while README's contribution section says outside contributions are not accepted. Without a license, default copyright applies: users technically may not copy/modify/redistribute — which contradicts the public installer story and creates ambiguity for anyone the operator invites (shared-jam collaborators).

OPTIONS TO PRESENT (with consequences, for the operator to choose): (a) MIT/Apache-2.0 — maximal adoption, others may fork/commercialize; Apache adds a patent grant. (b) PolyForm Noncommercial / BSL-style source-available — public reading + personal use, no commercial competition; not OSI open source, some users will pass. (c) AGPL-3.0 — open source with strong copyleft; commercial users mostly self-exclude. (d) Explicit all-rights-reserved notice — honest closed posture; the installer story should then say "source-visible, not open source". The no-outside-contributions stance is compatible with ALL options (license != contribution policy) but pairs naturally with (b)/(d); if (a)/(c), consider a CONTRIBUTING.md formalizing "PRs reviewed for illustration, not merged" as README already sketches.

EXECUTION ONCE DECIDED: add LICENSE file (or NOTICE for option d), README badge/section update, remove the Limitations line, note in CHANGELOG; if source-available, audit install.sh/docs wording for "open source" claims and correct them.

ACCEPTANCE: (1) operator decision recorded HERE with date and rationale; (2) LICENSE/NOTICE committed; (3) README + install docs consistent with the choice; (4) Limitations entry removed.

RISKS: none technical; the only failure mode is an agent "helpfully" picking one — hence this is type-decision and blocks nothing else in the plan.

MAINTAINER NOTES: This bead deliberately has no dependencies and nothing depends on it: the legal gap is real but must not bottleneck engineering. Surface it to the operator at the next session touching docs.
close_reason: Operator decision 2026-06-21: keep all-rights-reserved / no OSI license. README posture beads in the docs epic to state this explicitly.

comments.author: rob-ask-cascade
comments.text: OPERATOR DECISION 2026-06-21 (Rob, via ask-cascade): Keep the repository PRIVATE / all-rights-reserved for now. No OSI license file. Docs/README "Limitations" should explicitly state: source is all-rights-reserved and not yet open-source licensed. Revisit only if/when a public release is decided.

title: D1: reconcile posture docs with reality + break-glass recovery section
description:
OBJECTIVE: Reconcile the network-posture documentation with implemented reality — default posture, the SSH break-glass story, and recovery paths — resolving MODES D1.

BACKGROUND: MODES D1 (0.88): VISION/README posture claims drifted from the implemented default (TAILNET_ONLY_SSH behavior revised after the 2026-04-08 lockout incident; the transitional default is DEFENSIBLE but the docs do not tell that story). Since then, the tailnet-only lifecycle shipped fully (commits e063115, fa40bba, 9969c02: lockdown stage, posture-proof, exposure lint, docs/tailnet-only-lifecycle.md) — so D1 is now a DOCS-ONLY reconciliation: state the default, the why (incident history), the break-glass mechanics, and where each is verified.

REASONING: security posture docs that overstate hardening are worse than none — an operator who BELIEVES public SSH is closed behaves differently than one who knows the aperture story. Honest docs are also the audit trail for the posture-proof command's claims.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: (1) Verify current reality first (read box.py lockdown stage + 02-install-tailscale.sh + posture-proof): what is the actual default for new boxes today, what does lockdown close, what break-glass exists (DO console? recovery documented?). Write down WHAT IS, not what was planned. (2) Updates: VISION.md (posture paragraph matches reality + links lifecycle doc), README/docs networking sections (one consistent statement of default + pointer), docs/tailnet-only-lifecycle.md (add an explicit Break-glass & Recovery section if thin: lost-tailscale-key scenario, droplet console path, the documented recovery commands; the doc already covers recovery partially — extend, do not duplicate). (3) Add the 2026-04-08 incident as a dated design-rationale note (one paragraph — why the bootstrap aperture exists). (4) Cross-check posture-proof output fields against the doc's claims (each documented guarantee should map to a verifiable check; note any guarantee with no check as a candidate follow-up bead).

FILES: docs/VISION.md, docs/tailnet-only-lifecycle.md, README posture mentions, possibly AGENTS.md Network Posture section.

RISKS: documenting a gap that turns out to be a bug (if verification finds posture-proof NOT checking something docs claim — file a bug bead, do not paper over it in prose).

ACCEPTANCE: (1) one consistent posture statement across VISION/README/lifecycle doc/AGENTS (grep for contradictions); (2) break-glass section exists with concrete commands; (3) incident rationale dated; (4) every documented guarantee maps to a posture-proof check or a filed follow-up; (5) reviewed against a real box-status/posture-proof output (redacted) attached to the PR.

TESTING: docs-only; the guarantee->check mapping table in the PR is the verification artifact; link checker (README-restructure bead) covers references.

OBSERVABILITY: n/a.

MAINTAINER NOTES: The posture docs' job is to let the operator answer "what happens if tailscale dies on a locked-down box" without an archaeology session. Optimize for that reader.
close_reason: done

title: Onboarding demo: examples/first-box-demo.md with captured expected outputs
description:
OBJECTIVE: A runnable onboarding demo — examples/first-box-demo.md walking zero-to-focused-client on a local checkout with a public sample repo, every command paired with its expected output — doubling as a manual acceptance script for the first-box flow.

BACKGROUND: the README quickstart lists commands but not what success LOOKS like; the personal-client caveat box (clients are operator-private, fresh clones have none) is exactly where new operators stall — they run a CLIENT= command, get the "no client overlays attached" recovery message, and have no worked example showing the client-init -> focus arc end to end. The blueprints (git-repo-http-service-bootstrap) exist precisely for this but no doc exercises one publicly.

REASONING: a demo with expected outputs is simultaneously onboarding doc, regression oracle (outputs drift -> doc fails -> someone notices), and the honest answer to "what is this project like to use" for evaluators. For agents, it is few-shot training data for operating the box correctly.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: (1) Script of the demo: clone -> cp .env.example .env -> make render/doctor (show healthy output excerpts) -> runtime-sync -> client-init demo-client --blueprint git-repo-http-service-bootstrap --set PRIMARY_REPO_URL=<a tiny public sample repo — create skillbox-demo-app under the operator's GitHub or use an existing tiny public repo; pin by URL in the doc> --set SERVICE_COMMAND/BOOTSTRAP_COMMAND for a hello-world HTTP server -> focus demo-client -> status (show the live table) -> curl the service -> logs -> down -> the cleanup path. (2) Every command block followed by an expected-output block, NORMALIZED (paths as <ROOT>, timestamps elided) and dated with the version/commit it was captured at. (3) Cleanup section returns the checkout to pristine (remove the demo client, verify doctor green) — the demo must be safely re-runnable. (4) Honest timing notes (sync clones skill repos — first run is minutes). (5) Cross-link from README quickstart and docs/clients.md (coordinate with the README-restructure bead; whichever lands second wires links). (6) Capture outputs from a REAL run — no synthesized output; note any flaky lines as variable.

FILES: examples/first-box-demo.md (new), possibly a tiny public demo-app repo (operator action — flag in PR if created), README/docs links.

RISKS: output drift making the doc lie (the dated-capture convention + normalization bounds it; the demo is also the natural victim-first artifact when CLI output changes — acceptable, even desirable as a drift alarm); blueprint defaults changing (pin the blueprint name + --set keys used).

ACCEPTANCE: (1) a fresh operator (or agent) following the doc verbatim on a clean checkout reaches a curl-able demo service; (2) cleanup restores doctor-green pristine state; (3) every output block from a real captured run, dated; (4) linked from README quickstart; (5) re-running the demo after cleanup works (idempotency of the walkthrough).

TESTING: the demo IS a manual test; optionally extract its command list into a comment-block the e2e-smoke bead could someday consume (note only, no coupling now).

OBSERVABILITY: n/a.

MAINTAINER NOTES: When a CLI change breaks this doc, that is the doc WORKING — update the captures as part of the breaking change, the same discipline as goldens.

title: Run installer-workmanship on the public install.sh path
description:
NO-RAGRETS CONTRACT
Outcome: The public curl-pipe-bash installer is reviewed and, where reasonable, hardened through the installer-workmanship skill before the onboarding/docs epic treats the install path as polished.
Evidence: a checklist-backed installer audit exists; accepted fixes land with tests; install.sh help, offline archive, verification, proxy, quiet/no-gum, and failure paths are exercised or explicitly marked out of scope with rationale.
Failure avoided: a fresh operator's first command is a brittle installer that lacks clear failure recovery, supply-chain checks, proxy/offline support, or trustworthy final status.

SKILL TO RUN
Use installer-workmanship. Start with that skill's expected workflow and read its SKILL.md before work. Before changing install.sh, read the canonical reference installers if present:
- /dp/destructive_command_guard/install.sh
- /dp/remote_compilation_helper/install.sh
If either reference path is unavailable, record that as evidence and use the installer-workmanship checklist as the fallback rubric instead of improvising.

PLACEMENT AND SCOPE
This belongs under skillbox-docs-onboarding-epic-m19o because install.sh and the README quickstart are the first operator onboarding surface. Target:
- install.sh
- README.md install/quickstart sections and docs pages moved by the docs epic
- tests/test_install_script.py
- scripts/box.py release-install argument construction when it invokes install.sh on remote boxes
- scripts/06-upgrade-release.sh only where installer contract assumptions overlap upgrade verification
Do not turn this into a full release engineering rewrite. If the skill uncovers a large release pipeline gap, file a separate bead.

ACCEPTANCE
1. Audit install.sh against installer-workmanship non-negotiables: strict shell mode, cleanup trap, proxy handling, platform/preflight checks, locking, checksum verification, offline mode, build fallback, shell/agent integration, final summary, and uninstall/revert guidance.
2. Decide which gaps are in scope for this repo's installer contract and record non-goals explicitly.
3. Implement accepted fixes with focused tests, preserving existing CLI flags and documented workflows.
4. README/docs examples still match the installer flags and failure recovery text.
5. No secrets, local .env data, or operator-specific paths are committed.

VALIDATION COMMANDS
- bash install.sh --help
- python3 -m unittest tests.test_install_script
- python3 -m unittest tests.test_upgrade_release_script tests.test_box tests.test_box_lifecycle tests.test_box_refactor when remote install/upgrade args are touched
- shellcheck install.sh scripts/06-upgrade-release.sh if shellcheck is available; otherwise state that shellcheck was unavailable
- make doctor when docs/reconcile/install contract changes are broad enough

WRITES
Expected writes are limited to install.sh, README/docs install sections, focused installer/upgrade/box tests, and follow-up Beads. Avoid generated/runtime state. Do not run install.sh in a way that downloads, clones, builds containers, provisions boxes, or mutates infrastructure unless the operator explicitly approves that side effect.

MODEL ROUTE AND AUTHORITY
Codex gpt-5.5 owns orchestration, implementation, review, and final acceptance. Use a bounded worker only for an independent installer checklist audit if the diff becomes large. Claude Fable is not needed. Grok/Composer may only run narrow shell-test or doc consistency tasks with explicit validation and Codex review.

STOP CONDITION
Stop when either accepted installer fixes are implemented and verified, or the pass produces a concrete blocker/decision bead for any non-negotiable that cannot be satisfied safely in this repo. Do not close on a vague audit note.
notes: ROUTING: requires Claude Code skill runtime (/viral-product-score, /saas-cli-auth-flow, /installer-workmanship). Cannot be dispatched to codex workers. Needs claude pane.

title: Lube: auto-sync beads on git commit to eliminate per-session br sync ceremony
description:
CASS evidence: 120+ sessions contain manual 'br sync --flush-only' invocations across 500 recent sessions. Every session that touches beads ends with the same 3-step ceremony: br sync --flush-only, git add .beads/, git commit. This is pure mechanical friction.

No-ragrets success contract:
- Outcome: a pre-commit hook auto-runs 'br sync --flush-only' when .beads/beads.db exists, so agents never type the sync command manually
- Evidence: grep across 50 future sessions shows zero manual 'br sync --flush-only' invocations
- Failure avoided: agents forgetting to sync beads before commit, stale .beads/issues.jsonl diverging from SQLite

Lube unblocker: add a pre-commit hook (or git hook template) in the skillbox repo that detects .beads/beads.db and runs br sync --flush-only automatically. The hook should be idempotent, fast (<2s), and silent on success. Install it via make bootstrap-env or a dedicated make install-hooks target.
notes: Included in no-ragrets high-use weak-surface slate skillbox-high-use-weak-surfaces-no-ragrets-fxm8. User agreed this is a future pass candidate: remove repeated manual br sync ceremony while preserving explicit git staging/commit review.
close_reason: Pre-commit hook auto-syncs beads: .githooks/pre-commit, make install-hooks, wired into bootstrap-env

title: Add external network posture proof artifact
description: Add an operator-machine proof command/artifact for network posture. It should run from outside the box, inspect DO firewall when credentials exist, run public TCP negative probes, run Tailnet positive probes, and save a redacted JSON artifact suitable for release or handoff evidence. Model route and authority: Codex gpt-5.5 owns orchestration, system design, implementation, review, commit acceptance, and final say. Claude Opus is only for UX/docs visual review if a docs surface needs design judgment. Grok/Composer 2.5 may run bounded probes only when the validation commands and stronger-model review are explicit. Stop condition: do not treat server-local self-connects as public reachability proof.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: command records absolute timestamp, operator host, droplet id/ip, Tailnet identity, public probe results, Tailnet probe results, DO firewall summary, skipped/inconclusive rows, and exact commands used. Validation: tests cover timeout/refused/succeeded classification and token-redacted doctl output; docs show how to run without exposing secrets.
notes: No-ragrets contract. Outcome: posture proof is run from an external/operator vantage point and archived as redacted evidence. Evidence: timestamp, operator host, droplet id/ip, Tailnet identity, public negative probes, Tailnet positive probes, firewall summary, skipped/inconclusive rows, and exact commands. Failure avoided: server-local hairpin/self-connect tests masquerade as internet reachability proof.

title: Lube: auto-clear stale index-run.lock from dead cass indexer PIDs
description:
CASS evidence: 64 hits for agents encountering stale index-run.lock files from dead indexer PIDs. Experienced directly in this session: PID 125188 died but left lock behind, causing cass status/health to report stale and blocking subsequent cass operations. Agents had to manually identify the dead PID, confirm it was not running, and remove the lock files.

No-ragrets success contract:
- Outcome: sbp cass status auto-detects stale locks (PID dead per os.kill(pid, 0)), clears them, and reports the cleared lock in the payload. No manual intervention needed.
- Evidence: direct experience in this session + 64 cass hits showing the same pattern across sessions.
- Failure avoided: agents spending tool calls diagnosing a phantom lock, manually clearing files, and retrying operations.
- Acceptance: (1) dead PID locks are auto-cleared before health probes, (2) live PID locks are left alone, (3) cleared lock info is included in the status payload for transparency, (4) 12/12 tests pass including 2 new stale-lock tests.
close_reason: Fix applied: _clear_stale_index_lock() in sbp_cass.py auto-detects dead PIDs via os.kill(pid, 0) and clears lock+meta files. 12/12 tests pass.

title: Generated build000r skills clone divergence blocks runtime-sync
description: Evidence (2026-06-11): make runtime-sync in /srv/skillbox/repos/opensource/skillbox fails with SKILL_REPO_CLONE_FAILED because workspace/skill-repos/build000r-skills cannot fast-forward origin/main. git status reports main...origin/main [ahead 1, behind 28]; local ahead commit is 140921e docs(smart): require read before edit. This generated skill repo is local/operator state, and hardening should not reset, rebase, merge, or discard it implicitly. Impact: runtime-sync cannot repair managed skill install drift, so make doctor/runtime-manager-doctor can continue to report stale installed skills until the operator chooses a merge/rebase/archive/reset policy for this generated clone. Acceptance: workspace/skill-repos/build000r-skills is reconciled by an explicit operator decision, make runtime-sync exits 0, and make doctor no longer reports SKILL_REPO_CLONE_FAILED or skill-repo-install drift caused by stale managed homes.
close_reason: Reconciled by explicit decision: clone-only commit 140921e (smart/SKILL.md Read-before-Edit) preserved into primary /srv/skillbox/repos/skills as 32ceea3 via git am, managed clone reset to origin/main (d7f6bf7). make runtime-sync exits 0, doctor: 0 failing (was 2), no SKILL_REPO_CLONE_FAILED. Remaining skill-repo-install warn (undeclared extras) tracked in skillbox-repair-host-migrated-skill-links-1bld.

title: No-ragrets: high-use weak surfaces agent friction slate
description:
Future vantage point: after several more agent sessions, the operator is glad these high-use weak surfaces were converted from repeated manual ceremonies into one-command recovery paths or explicit safe policies.

Scope: this umbrella captures the agreed future pass after the doctor fix. It includes sbp skill-visibility recovery, Beads sync ceremony removal, agent-ops brain intent recovery, snap CLI consistency, and a full CLI ergonomics pass. It also records the revised CASS decision: do not ship inline auto-rebuild during agent work. CASS rebuilds should be coordinated with the operator/local machine, run off-hours at 5am PST, and immediate evidence needs should fall back to recent logs/local transcript paths rather than rebuilding the index mid-task.

No-ragrets success contract:
- Outcome: repeated high-use agent ceremonies are reduced to one command, one structured recovery payload, or an explicit non-actionable blocked policy.
- Evidence: future sessions show fewer manual sbp recalibrate/activate, br sync --flush-only, CASS status/rebuild, UNKNOWN_NODE retry, and snap usage-error retries.
- Failure avoided: agents waste tool calls on rituals, mutate local/operator state at the wrong time, or abandon the intended tool because recovery is too noisy.

Graph rule: concrete remediation beads carry implementation details. This epic is complete only when children are closed or explicitly blocked/superseded with clear operator-policy evidence.

description: Make host-level SSH lockdown an explicit defense-in-depth path after Tailscale enrollment. scripts/02-install-tailscale.sh already has TAILNET_ONLY_SSH; future work should make the managed-box lifecycle pass it intentionally, verify UFW/nft state, and avoid relying on cloud firewall alone. Model route and authority: Codex gpt-5.5 owns orchestration, system design, implementation, review, commit acceptance, and final say. Claude Opus is only for UX/docs visual review if a docs surface needs design judgment. Grok/Composer 2.5 may run bounded probes only when the validation commands and stronger-model review are explicit. Failure avoided: removing a cloud firewall cannot silently expose sshd bound to 0.0.0.0:22.

Validation: shell/unit tests inspect generated sshd/UFW behavior without locking out the test runner; docs call out operator-safe live sequencing.

title: Reconcile managed default skill install drift
description:
Reality check: `make doctor` and `make dev-sanity` fail `skill-repo-install` for managed default-skills drift involving `divide-and-conquer`, `domain-planner`, `domain-reviewer`, and `domain-scaffolder` in both Claude and Codex installs.

Fix direction: use supported Skillbox sync/doctor paths to reconcile installed managed skills, or record the exact blocker if the local checkout cannot safely mutate the install state. Keep this repo-local and do not prune unrelated global skill repos.

writes:
  - .skillbox-state/<home>/.claude/skills/**
  - .skillbox-state/<home>/.codex/skills/**

  - Stop before deleting or pruning anything outside managed Skillbox install roots.

  - Do not run broad global skill cleanup.
global_constraints:
  - Use supported Skillbox sync/doctor paths only; do not prune unrelated global skill repos.
  - Do not edit secrets, .env, .env.box, workspace/secrets, or client overlays.
  - Do not commit; integration lead owns final commit decision.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
* Supported sync dry-run has been previewed and recorded.
* Managed default skill install drift is reconciled through supported Skillbox paths or blocked with exact unsupported/unsafe evidence.
* make doctor and make dev-sanity no longer fail on stale installs, or exact residual blocker is recorded.

  - make dev-sanity

Residual skill-repo-install warning is limited to extra undeclared managed skills agent-ergonomics-and-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools and ga4; supported skill prune dry-run had zero actions for this managed state, so no manual prune was performed.

title: Fix operator booking MCP customer-name validation
description:
Root cause: `.env-manager/mcp_server.py` validates any argument key named `name` as a restricted identifier. `skillbox_operator_booking` uses `name` as the customer display name, so valid booking payloads can be rejected before they reach the manage.py mapping path.

Fix direction: make identifier validation tool-schema-aware or restrict it to true client/profile/service/task/skill identifiers. Preserve the identifier guard for tools such as `skillbox_overlay` where `name` is an overlay identifier.
design:

stop_rules:

global_constraints:
  - Do not edit secrets, .env, .env.box, workspace/secrets, or generated runtime state.
  - Do not commit; integration lead owns final commit decision.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
* skillbox_operator_booking accepts a normal customer display name without identifier validation failure.
* A true identifier argument such as skillbox_overlay name still rejects unsafe input.
* Focused and adjacent MCP server tests pass or an unrelated blocker is recorded.

title: Fix up/down dependency graph recovery for unhealthy already-running services
description:
Context: The operator hit `sbp up` from <workspace>/cycle-chef and got spaps + htma_server reported as already-running while the API summary showed both health URLs down. This is not only a Cycle Chef issue; it is an upstream local-runtime dependency graph contract issue for any client overlay using managed services, depends_on, and healthchecks.

Problem: runtime_ops._start_service currently returns already-running as soon as a managed pid file resolves to a live pid. It does this before checking the declared healthcheck. That lets an unhealthy live process satisfy a dependency edge and makes `up` look successful while downstream services start against dead APIs. Text rendering also prints bootstrap task rows as unknown when local-runtime summaries use status instead of result.

Success contract:
- A live pid with a failing declared healthcheck is not treated as successful already-running.
- `up` either repairs the service through the normal declared command path after clearing stale/unhealthy runtime state, or returns a structured failed/blocked result that stops the graph and points to status/logs/restart.
- Healthy pre-existing HTTP/port/process/path services are still reused without spawning duplicates.
- Dependency graph startup does not continue after a dependency fails or times out.
- Text output for bootstrap tasks renders status/result clearly, never harmless unknown for planned/ready/completed rows.

Validation target: focused unittest coverage for runtime_ops/start_services, local-runtime up graph failure behavior, text rendering task rows, plus python3 -m unittest for touched test modules. Link evidence back to skillbox-config runtime normalization bead skillbox-config-sbp-up-runtime-tree-m9z.3.
close_reason: Implemented unhealthy live-pid recovery, dependency blocking, task status rendering, and focused validation; dev-sanity caveat recorded for unrelated stale skill installs.

comments.author: b
comments.text: Implemented upstream local-runtime lifecycle fix. Code changes: runtime_ops now checks declared service health before reusing a live managed pid; unhealthy live-pid services are stopped and restarted through the declared command path when possible; failed dependencies now cause downstream services to return result=blocked instead of starting; workflows maps blocked to LOCAL_RUNTIME_START_BLOCKED; text renderer uses status when bootstrap summaries do not carry result. Regression coverage added in tests/test_local_runtime.py and tests/test_text_renderers.py. Validation passed: py_compile for touched files; python3 -m unittest tests.test_local_runtime.BridgeBackedTaskTests.test_start_services_restarts_unhealthy_live_pid tests.test_local_runtime.BridgeBackedTaskTests.test_start_services_blocks_dependents_after_dependency_failure tests.test_text_renderers.LocalRuntimeErrorRendererTests.test_service_actions_task_rows_prefer_status_when_result_absent; python3 -m unittest tests.test_runtime_manager tests.test_text_renderers; python3 -m unittest tests.test_local_runtime; python3 -m unittest tests.test_runtime_hotspot_units tests.test_text_renderers; git diff --check. comments.author: b

Fixed timeout merge order and added regression test_start_services_times_out_when_process_lives_but_health_stays_down. title: Make box status enforce network posture policy
description: Upgrade box status or add box security-status so it evaluates desired network posture. Public SSH ok should be a failure for tailnet_only boxes, public app ports should be failures unless explicitly declared, and Tailnet-only app ports should be shown as intended private access. Model route and authority: Codex gpt-5.5 owns orchestration, system design, implementation, review, commit acceptance, and final say. Claude Opus is only for UX/docs visual review if a docs surface needs design judgment. Grok/Composer 2.5 may run bounded probes only when the validation commands and stronger-model review are explicit. Failure avoided: status prints facts but leaves agents to infer whether they are secure.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: JSON status includes policy_status, violations, cloud_firewall_status, host_firewall_status, public_probe_status, tailnet_probe_status, and app_exposure_status; text output names pass/fail plainly. Validation: mocked network checks prove public SSH reachable => violation for tailnet_only, public timeout => pass, and explicit public exposure => documented exception.
notes: No-ragrets contract. Outcome: box status produces policy pass/fail, not just observed network facts. Evidence: JSON/text status fields for cloud firewall, host firewall, public probes, Tailnet probes, app exposure, and violations with mocked reachability tests. Failure avoided: agents see 0.0.0.0 or a successful probe and must guess whether the box is secure.
close_reason: Integrated evaluate_posture_violations into box_health (box.py:3614-3636), added network_posture/cloud_firewall_id/posture_violations to status JSON, added violation-driven next_actions, updated print_box_status_text to show posture line and violations. 3 new tests.

title: Run saas-cli-auth-flow on the SPAPS token/auth boundary
description:
NO-RAGRETS CONTRACT
Outcome: Skillbox has a concrete, security-reviewed route for SPAPS-backed CLI auth/token renewal instead of another ad hoc token-refresh patch.
Evidence: the saas-cli-auth-flow skill is applied to the SPAPS/token boundary; the result names which repo owns each change, which auth tiers are in scope, what is intentionally delegated to SPAPS/buildooor, and what public skillbox tests/docs prove.
Failure avoided: agents keep hitting expired-token loops or wire bearer tokens into logs/env/files without a clear PKCE/device-code/refresh/secure-storage contract.

SKILL TO RUN
Use saas-cli-auth-flow. Start with that skill's required progress marker and read its SKILL.md before work. Apply it to the Skillbox/SPAPS auth boundary, especially:
- existing bead skillbox-spaps-token-refresh-iuk and its headless token-renewal evidence
- workspace/client-blueprints/git-repo-http-service-bootstrap-spaps-auth.yaml
- README.md first-box/SPAPS auth sections and .env-manager/README.md blueprint docs
- scripts/operator_mcp_server.py default first-box blueprint wiring
- tests touching SPAPS auth/RBAC fixture expectations, operator booking, swimmers token exposure, and blueprint/runtime wiring

PLACEMENT AND SCOPE
This is intentionally a boundary task, not a blind implementation task. First decide where the real auth code belongs:
- If the change belongs in SPAPS/buildooor CLI/server repos, produce exact external implementation beads or comments and update skillbox-spaps-token-refresh-iuk with the dependency/result.
- If skillbox only owns blueprint/docs/runtime wiring, implement that local contract here and leave the token mint/refresh protocol to the owning repo.
- Do not invent a new auth system in this repo just to satisfy the skill checklist.

ACCEPTANCE
1. Protocol/boundary note covers browser PKCE, manual/headless path, RFC 8628 device-code path, token refresh/revoke, secure credential storage, CI/API-key fallback, and redaction/logging rules, with each item marked skillbox-owned, SPAPS-owned, or out of scope.
2. Any local changes preserve the Tailnet/local-auth posture and do not expose auth surfaces beyond intended loopback/private boundaries.
3. The existing SPAPS token-refresh bead is related/updated with the outcome so future agents know whether to work in this repo or an external repo.
4. Tests/docs prove the local skillbox contract that remains in scope.
5. Any blocker names the missing repo/access/endpoint and the exact next command or bead to pursue.

VALIDATION COMMANDS
- br show skillbox-spaps-token-refresh-iuk --json
- python3 -m unittest tests.test_operator_booking tests.test_swimmers_script tests.test_swimmers_launch when touched
- python3 -m unittest tests.test_runtime_manager when blueprint/runtime model wiring changes
- python3 -m unittest discover -s tests when auth-related wiring touches shared runtime code
- make doctor when docs/manifests/blueprints change

WRITES
Expected local writes are limited to docs/protocol notes, blueprint/runtime wiring, focused tests, and Beads/comments that route external work. Do not commit tokens, secrets, local auth artifacts, or generated client state. Do not call live auth endpoints or mutate production/server auth state unless the operator explicitly approves that external action.

MODEL ROUTE AND AUTHORITY
Codex gpt-5.5 owns orchestration, protocol-boundary judgment, implementation review, and final authority. Use one focused reviewer/worker only if independent security critique would materially change the boundary. Claude Fable is not needed. Grok/Composer may only handle bounded doc/test edits with explicit validation and Codex review.

STOP CONDITION
Stop when the ownership boundary and next executable auth work are unambiguous and any local skillbox-owned changes are verified. If all implementation belongs outside this repo, close only after external beads/comments are created and the local graph points to them.
notes: ROUTING: requires Claude Code skill runtime (/viral-product-score, /saas-cli-auth-flow, /installer-workmanship). Cannot be dispatched to codex workers. Needs claude pane.

description: Lead-driven divide-and-conquer wave. Seed: docs/status/skillbox-profiling-refactor-candidates-2026-05-28.md + skillbox-idea-slate-2026-05-28.md. Phase 0 (security redaction) already landed at 7b11ef9.

Closed concrete child work: context drift goldens and worker broker smoke artifacts kept and tested; pressure warning consolidation landed with focused validation; evidence assembler was reviewed as already correctly factored with zero edits; event-feed redaction fixed and tested; worker runtime flake hardened with five solo passes; install-test pulse leak duplicate closed through push-readiness fix. No remaining ready wave-4 push blocker.

title: Review Skillbox repo Claude-only MCP extras cm dcg fwc
description:
Audit finding from sbp mcp in opensource/skillbox: expected servers are skillbox and skillbox-operator, but .mcp.json also exposes Claude-only cm, dcg, and fwc while Codex config does not. This is not a pdsmvp blocker, but it is repo-local MCP parity drift that should be reviewed deliberately.

Scope:
- Inspect .mcp.json and .codex/config.toml plus the runtime/skillbox overlay expectations.
- Decide whether cm/dcg/fwc are intentional Claude-only local extras, should be mirrored to Codex, or should be removed/disabled.
- Do not broaden into global/fleet MCP cleanup.

Acceptance:
- Decision is recorded with file evidence.
- If config changes are made, sbp mcp --format json and sbp recalibrate report the intended parity state.
- make dev-sanity or focused MCP tests run if a runtime surface changes.
close_reason: Reviewed and recorded decision: cm/dcg/fwc are ignored Claude-local extras; no tracked MCP config change needed.

Evidence: sbp mcp expected_servers are skillbox and skillbox-operator; both Claude/Codex have those, and only .mcp.json has cm/dcg/fwc. .mcp.json and .codex/config.toml are ignored local config. The cm/dcg/fwc launch binaries are absent under /home/sandbox/.local/bin and /Users/b/.local/bin in this run. title: Add poll-until helper or teach agents to use Monitor for background tasks
description:
Friction: Agents hand-roll bespoke for/while loops with sleep and grep to poll for background task completion. Found in 3+ sessions: session f2053074 (7 distinct poll loops at lines 288-294, 340, 407, 481, 511, 529, 554), session 7dd38cb6 (4 distinct loops at lines 249, 283, 545, 669), and session d1664698 (loop at line 269). Each is fragile, burns tool calls, and sometimes gets rejected by the user (f2053074 L295).

Cass evidence: 11+ distinct hand-rolled poll loops across 3 sessions. Pattern: launch background task, write custom sleep/grep loop, poll output file, sometimes get rejected.

Lube fix (smallest durable unblocker): Add a poll-until shell helper to the skillbox agent toolkit that accepts a condition command and timeout, or surface guidance in AGENTS.md to use the Monitor tool (which streams events from background processes) instead of manual sleep-poll loops.

No-ragrets contract:
- Outcome: agents have a standard way to wait for background tasks without hand-rolling poll loops.
- Evidence: 11+ bespoke loops across sessions, each burning 2-5 tool calls.
- Failure avoided: agent writes fragile poll loop, user rejects it, agent rewrites, wastes a turn.
- Acceptance: (1) AGENTS.md or agent toolkit includes poll-until guidance, (2) at least one session uses Monitor or the helper instead of manual polling.
close_reason: Added Background Task Polling section to AGENTS.md with guidance to use Monitor tool or run_in_background instead of hand-rolled sleep/grep loops.

title: Port guard: five-layer enforcement stack from the June duel
description:
EPIC: Implement the five-layer port-guard stack that the 2026-06-11 dueling-wizards round (Claude Opus, Codex gpt-5.5, Grok — DUELING_WIZARDS_REPORT_PORT_GUARD.md) converged on independently, so dev services can no longer bind wrong ports, auto-hop, or accumulate as rogue listeners.

PROBLEM EVIDENCE: at duel time the live box showed four duplicate Vite servers (5173/5174/5176/5177), one on 0.0.0.0 violating tailnet_only posture. Agents bypass `sbp up` with direct `npm run dev`; Vite silently auto-increments ports.

CONSENSUS BUILD ORDER (verbatim from the duel): Phase 0 port registry (foundation, "not a ranked idea, a prerequisite") -> Phase 1 dev-command intercept via PATH shims + PreToolUse hook (avg score 810) -> Phase 2 post-bind verification in service start (763-845) -> Phase 3 pulse rogue-listener sentinel (745-795, only layer covering ALL bypass paths) -> Phase 4 repo port contracts (.skillbox-port.env + strictPort, 585-628) -> Phase 5 optional eBPF cage, DEFERRED behind telemetry.

KILLED IDEAS — do not resurrect: nftables port cage (filters packets, not bind(); formally retracted by two models), LD_PRELOAD bind guard (trivially bypassed), proxy-owned canonical ports (masks the problem; contradicts the 2026-06-04 port-per-app pivot).

SUCCESS CRITERIA (Grok, endorsed 3-way): (1) `npm run dev` in tmux blocked in one turn with the exact runtime command; (2) full-path bypass crashes on strictPort or is reaped within one pulse interval; (3) no silent port hops; (4) no multi-day rogue accumulation; (5) no surviving 0.0.0.0 dev listeners under tailnet_only.

Exit: all five criteria encoded as automated regression tests and passing; telemetry counters inform the Phase-5 decision bead.
close_reason: All five port-guard layers and the regression/telemetry proof bead are implemented, verified, and pushed.

title: Port registry: derived port->owner contract + ports CLI + doctor checks
description:
OBJECTIVE: Build the machine-readable port registry — the single source of truth mapping every declared port to its owning service, profile, client, and source declaration — plus CLI/doctor surfaces over it. This is Phase 0 of the port-guard stack; every later phase consumes it.

BACKGROUND: Ports today are scattered facts: runtime.yaml service healthchecks (http://localhost:8000/health), ingress_routes listeners, ~15 SKILLBOX_*_PORT env keys (.env.example), client overlay services, and docs/tailnet-ingress.md prose tables. Nothing enforces uniqueness or detects collisions; the duel rated this the non-negotiable prerequisite of all enforcement.

REASONING: you cannot block a wrong bind until "right port" is a queryable fact with provenance. The registry also upgrades agent context: generated CLAUDE.md can state exactly which port belongs to which service.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: (1) New module .env-manager/runtime_manager/port_registry.py building a registry view from the resolved runtime model: entries {port, owner_id, owner_kind (service|ingress|env_surface), client, profiles, bind_scope (loopback|tailnet|wildcard), source (file+key), protocol}. Parse ports from healthcheck targets, ingress listeners, declared `port`/env fields; tolerate unparseable health targets with a warning entry. (2) New CLI: `manage.py ports` (list, --format json/text) and `manage.py ports --resolve <service-id>`; register in command_registry.py (tier 2, surface cli+mcp, mcp_tool skillbox_ports, side_effect none, risk low) per AGENTS.md rules for new agent-facing commands. (3) Doctor checks: PORT_COLLISION (two active-scope owners, same port), PORT_WILDCARD_BIND (0.0.0.0 declared while box posture is tailnet_only), PORT_UNDECLARED_RESERVED (port inside a declared reserved range with no owner). (4) Context rendering: add a Ports table to generated agent context so in-box agents see the contract without asking. (5) Expose port attributes on service nodes in the agent ops graph.

FILES: runtime_manager/port_registry.py (new), scripts/lib/runtime_model.py (port extraction helpers), cli.py, command_registry.py, .env-manager/mcp_server.py (mirror), context_rendering.py, validation/doctor wiring, tests/test_agent_ops_command_registry.py goldens.

RISKS: health targets without ports (cfo declares `localhost:8050` w/o scheme; some checks are path_exists) — extraction must be conservative, never guess; client overlays loaded only when client active — registry is scope-aware, collisions checked within the ACTIVE scope plus a cross-client advisory mode.

ACCEPTANCE: (1) `manage.py ports --format json` lists every port declared in the active scope with owner + source; (2) fixture with duplicate port -> doctor fails PORT_COLLISION naming both declarations; (3) wildcard fixture under tailnet_only -> PORT_WILDCARD_BIND; (4) capabilities payload includes ports command; (5) generated context contains the ports table; (6) registry golden added to agent-ops goldens.

TESTING: unit tests for extraction (each source kind, malformed targets), collision/wildcard doctor fixtures, registry golden, MCP mirror smoke test, context drift golden regenerated deliberately.

OBSERVABILITY: doctor findings carry source provenance; ports command is read-only.

MAINTAINER NOTES: Keep the registry derivational — it must never become a second place to declare ports. If a port is not derivable from existing manifests, the fix is to enrich the manifest schema, not to hand-edit the registry.
close_reason: Phase 0 complete: port_registry.py builds scope-aware port->owner view with provenance (conservative extraction, never guesses ports); manage.py ports CLI + skillbox_ports MCP; PORT_COLLISION/PORT_WILDCARD_BIND/PORT_UNDECLARED_RESERVED doctor checks + cross-client advisory; context ports table + graph node attrs; 98 focused tests green

title: Dev-command intercept: PreToolUse hook + PATH shims for dev servers
description:
OBJECTIVE: Intercept dev-server launches that bypass the runtime: a PreToolUse Bash hook plus PATH shims for npm/pnpm/yarn/vite/next that block direct `dev` invocations inside managed repos and answer with the exact runtime command to use instead. Duel Phase 1, highest average score (810).

BACKGROUND: The observed failure mode is agents running `npm run dev` directly in tmux panes, spawning duplicate Vite servers on hopped ports. guard-destructive-op.sh proves the PreToolUse hook template works (matcher -> structured block). Nothing intercepts dev commands today.

REASONING: the cheapest moment to stop a rogue listener is before the process exists, with a message that teaches the contract ("use: python3 .env-manager/manage.py up --client X --service Y"). Shims catch full-path/tmux bypasses that never traverse the agent harness hook.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: (1) scripts/guard-dev-port.sh PreToolUse hook: match Bash tool calls whose command contains dev-server signatures (npm run dev, pnpm dev, yarn dev, vite, next dev, astro dev — keep the list in one table); resolve cwd -> managed repo via the port registry/runtime model; if the repo maps to a covered service, exit 2 with: owning service id, declared port, exact up command, and the escape hatch. Unmanaged repos pass through. (2) Shim directory (e.g. ${SKILLBOX_HOME_ROOT}/.local/skillbox-shims) with tiny wrappers for the same binaries: when SKILLBOX_MANAGED_RUN=1 (exported by runtime_ops service launch) they exec the real binary; otherwise same registry lookup + block message; installed by `make wrappers-install` and prepended to PATH by focus/context env. (3) Escape hatch: SKILLBOX_PORT_GUARD=off bypasses both layers, loudly logged — operators stay sovereign. (4) Register the hook in .claude/settings.json; document the codex-side equivalent limitation honestly.

FILES: scripts/guard-dev-port.sh (new), shim templates (new, under scripts/ or .env-manager/assets/), Makefile (wrappers-install), runtime_ops.py (export SKILLBOX_MANAGED_RUN for launched services), context/focus PATH wiring, .claude/settings.json, docs.

RISKS: false positives on `npm run dev` in a repo whose service is NOT covered (must pass through); shims must never break non-dev subcommands (npm install) — wrappers inspect argv and only gate the dev table; PATH injection must not leak into unrelated host shells (scope to in-box agent env and focus-generated env only).

ACCEPTANCE: (1) hook blocks `npm run dev` in a covered repo with remediation text containing the literal up command; (2) `npm install` and dev in uncovered repos pass; (3) shim blocks full-path bypass in tmux (no hook present); (4) SKILLBOX_MANAGED_RUN=1 passes; (5) SKILLBOX_PORT_GUARD=off passes and logs; (6) shellcheck clean.

TESTING: shell-harness tests (existing test_operational_scripts.py pattern) covering the matrix above; unit test that runtime_ops exports the managed-run marker; wrappers-install idempotency test.

OBSERVABILITY: every block/bypass appends a structured line (tool, cwd, matched signature, verdict) to logs/runtime/runtime.log so Phase-6 telemetry can count interceptions.

MAINTAINER NOTES: The block message IS the product — it converts a frustrated bypass into a taught contract. Keep it under 10 lines, lead with the exact command.
close_reason: Implemented dev-command guard hook, shims, runtime marker, and tests

title: Post-bind verification: enforce declared ports in service start/reuse
description:
OBJECTIVE: After the runtime starts (or adopts) a service, verify the process actually listens on its declared port; on mismatch, stop it and return a structured LOCAL_RUNTIME_PORT_MISMATCH error with observed-vs-declared and remediation. Duel Phase 2 — the sharpest single code-gap fix (scores 763-845).

BACKGROUND: runtime_ops.py start paths (_start_service / _healthy_service_start_result / _reused_existing_service_result) treat "process up + healthcheck passed" as success. Vite's auto-hop defeats this: the healthcheck URL on the declared port can even hit a DIFFERENT pre-existing listener while the new process binds port+1 — verified as a real code-path miss during the duel.

REASONING: this is the only layer that catches a wrong bind by a service the runtime itself launched, closing the loop between declaration (registry) and reality (kernel listener table).

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: (1) After health-wait, resolve the listener set of the service's process tree (pid + descendants) by parsing `ss -tlnp` output or /proc/net/tcp+/proc/<pid>/fd (stdlib-only; prefer ss with /proc fallback; both behind one helper for testability). (2) Compare against the registry's declared port(s) for the service: exact match -> success enriched with verified_port; bound elsewhere -> stop the started process group, return LOCAL_RUNTIME_PORT_MISMATCH {declared, observed, owner_of_observed?, next_actions:[free the port / fix repo config / see .skillbox-port.env]}; bound nowhere after grace (non-listening task-like service) -> only enforce when the service declares a port. (3) Reuse path: when adopting an existing healthy process, run the same verification before reporting reuse. (4) Add a small retry window (e.g. 3x1s) for slow binds before judging. (5) New error code registered alongside LOCAL_RUNTIME_* codes in scripts/lib/runtime_model.py.

FILES: runtime_manager/runtime_ops.py, scripts/lib/runtime_model.py (error code), runtime_manager/port_registry.py (lookup helper), text_renderers.py (human rendering), tests/test_runtime_manager.py.

RISKS: services legitimately binding multiple ports (declare-all in registry; mismatch only when declared set ∩ observed set is empty); IPv6-only binds (parse both tcp/tcp6); containers/PID namespaces — in-box this is same-namespace, document that limit; killing on mismatch must use the same managed-stop path as `down` (no orphaned children).

ACCEPTANCE: (1) fixture service that binds declared port -> up succeeds, payload includes verified_port; (2) fixture binding port+1 -> service stopped, LOCAL_RUNTIME_PORT_MISMATCH with observed port; (3) reuse-adoption of a wrong-port process -> same error, not silent reuse; (4) non-port services unaffected; (5) error code documented in README error tables.

TESTING: unit tests with fake listener resolvers (no real sockets) for the decision matrix; one integration-style test using a real ephemeral python socket server to validate the ss/proc parser on Linux; regression: full lifecycle tests green.

OBSERVABILITY: runtime.log line on every mismatch (service, declared, observed, action=stopped); pulse/status surfaces last verification result per service.

MAINTAINER NOTES: Resist the temptation to "just warn" — the duel consensus was explicit that silent hops are the disease. Warn-only mode is acceptable solely via SKILLBOX_PORT_GUARD=off.
close_reason: Implemented post-bind declared-port verification with mismatch errors and tests

title: Pulse rogue-listener sentinel: scan, classify, grace, reap
description:
OBJECTIVE: Add a rogue-listener sentinel to the pulse daemon: every cycle, scan listening sockets, classify them against the port registry and managed-process ancestry, and reap unauthorized dev-server listeners after a grace window. Duel Phase 3 — the only layer covering ALL bypass paths (scores 745-795).

BACKGROUND: Shims and hooks can be bypassed (raw node invocation, processes started before guard install, tmux panes predating focus). The duel's problem statement showed four accumulated Vite rogues; a sentinel would have reaped all four. pulse.py already owns the reconcile loop, state snapshot (pulse.state.json), and runtime.log event stream — the sentinel is a natural new check.

REASONING: continuous observation beats point-in-time checks (Design Stance #8); the sentinel converts the registry from advice into an invariant the box maintains about itself.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: (1) pulse.py _scan_rogue_listeners(): enumerate listeners (same ss/proc helper as the post-bind bead — share it); classify each: registry-declared port owned by its managed process -> ok; system allowlist (sshd, tailscaled, docker-proxy, ports < 1024 unless dev-signature) -> ok; process whose ancestry chain includes a managed service launch -> ok; otherwise candidate-rogue. (2) Dev-server signature heuristic (process name/cmdline contains node+vite/next/webpack-dev-server etc.) decides enforcement class: signatures -> reap after grace; non-signatures -> report-only (never kill an operator's ad-hoc python -m http.server without explicit opt-in). (3) Grace window 15s (config SKILLBOX_PORT_SENTINEL_GRACE_SECONDS) so HMR restarts and legitimate quick rebinds survive. (4) Modes via SKILLBOX_PORT_SENTINEL=off|observe|enforce, default observe for two releases then enforce — record the flip as an explicit follow-up in the telemetry bead. (5) Wildcard binds (0.0.0.0) on tailnet_only posture escalate to enforce regardless of mode? No — keep observe honest, but mark them CRITICAL in events. (6) Every action -> runtime.log event + counters in pulse.state.json {rogues_seen, rogues_reaped, by_signature}.

FILES: .env-manager/pulse.py, shared listener helper (from post-bind bead), .env.example (new envs), tests/test_pulse*.py, docs (pulse section + tailnet lifecycle exposure rules).

RISKS: killing the wrong thing is the nightmare — ancestry resolution must be conservative (when in doubt, observe + report); PID reuse between scan and kill (re-verify cmdline immediately before SIGTERM); SIGTERM->wait->SIGKILL escalation with bounded wait; pulse cycle budget (scan must stay <100ms typical — cache /proc walks).

ACCEPTANCE: (1) fixture rogue with dev signature in enforce mode -> SIGTERM'd after grace, event logged with reason; (2) same in observe mode -> reported, alive; (3) managed service listener never flagged; (4) allowlisted system daemons never flagged; (5) pulse-status shows sentinel counters; (6) re-verification prevents PID-reuse kills (unit-tested with a swapped cmdline).

TESTING: unit tests with faked listener/process tables covering the full classification matrix; integration test with a real disposable socket process in observe mode; chaos overlap: sentinel + restart-backoff interplay test (a reaped managed-lookalike must not trigger restart storms).

OBSERVABILITY: structured events (kind=port_sentinel, action, pid, port, signature, reason); counters in pulse.state.json; status/stewardship surfaces totals.

MAINTAINER NOTES: Default-observe is deliberate: trust is earned with two weeks of clean telemetry, then enforce becomes default. The flip decision lives in the port-guard telemetry bead — do not flip ad hoc.
close_reason: Implemented pulse rogue-listener sentinel with observe/enforce modes, grace tracking, PID re-verification, counters, docs, and tests.

title: Repo port contracts: generated .skillbox-port.env + strictPort adoption
description:
OBJECTIVE: Generate per-repo port contracts from the registry — a gitignored .skillbox-port.env in each covered repo plus strictPort guidance — so even a full-bypass dev launch crashes on the wrong port instead of silently hopping. Duel Phase 4 (scores 585-628), the complement that turns hop-prone tools honest.

BACKGROUND: Vite/Next auto-increment when the desired port is busy. With strictPort:true (Vite) / fixed PORT env (Next, Node servers), a busy declared port is a loud crash — exactly what we want once layers 1-3 exist to explain WHY. The duel was explicit: env-file + overlay-synced contracts, NOT in-repo source edits (skillbox must not commit to client repos).

REASONING: contracts-in-env make the registry's truth ambient: every tool that honors PORT/.env conventions inherits the declaration without skillbox patching repo source.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: (1) During focus/bridge/sync for a client, for each covered service with a declared port, write <repo>/.skillbox-port.env containing PORT=<declared>, HOST=<bind addr per posture>, SKILLBOX_SERVICE_ID=<id>; file is generated state — ensure .gitignore coverage via a doctor advisory (skillbox cannot edit client repo .gitignore; the advisory tells the operator the exact line to add). (2) Document (and template into overlay scaffolds) the one-line repo-side adoption: vite --strictPort with port from env, or `port: Number(process.env.PORT), strictPort: true` snippet; blueprints used by client-init get the snippet included. (3) Doctor advisory check PORT_CONTRACT_UNADOPTED (warn-level): covered web service whose repo lacks .skillbox-port.env consumption hints — heuristic, warn-only, suppressible per-service in the overlay. (4) Regeneration is idempotent and atomic (temp+rename).

FILES: runtime_manager (env bridge / focus path writing the files), overlay blueprint templates, doctor advisory, docs (local runtime profiles section), .env.example notes.

RISKS: repos that already manage PORT themselves (contract file must compose, not fight — document precedence and never overwrite repo-owned .env files; we only own the .skillbox-port.env name); generated files in repos that get committed accidentally (advisory + docs; name is distinctive and greppable).

ACCEPTANCE: (1) focus on a fixture client writes .skillbox-port.env per covered service with registry-matching values; (2) regeneration after a port change updates the file; (3) doctor advisory fires for an unadopted covered web service and is suppressible; (4) overlay blueprint scaffolds include the strictPort snippet; (5) files never written into non-covered repos.

TESTING: unit tests for generation (values, atomicity, idempotency, suppression); blueprint scaffold test asserting snippet presence; doctor advisory fixture tests.

OBSERVABILITY: sync/focus step output lists contract files written; doctor advisory carries the exact adoption snippet.

MAINTAINER NOTES: This layer alone is weak (someone deletes the env file) — it exists because combined with the sentinel it makes the WRONG path crash loudly and the RIGHT path effortless. Resist expanding it into in-repo source patching; that boundary was a duel decision.
close_reason: Implemented generated repo port contracts, strictPort blueprint guidance, and doctor advisories.

title: Port-guard regression suite + telemetry: encode the duel's 5 criteria
description:
OBJECTIVE: Encode the duel's five success criteria as an automated regression suite plus telemetry aggregation, producing the evidence that decides whether the deferred Phase-5 eBPF cage is ever needed and when the sentinel flips observe->enforce by default.

BACKGROUND: The duel ended with measurable criteria: (1) `npm run dev` blocked in one turn with exact remediation; (2) full bypass crashes via strictPort or is reaped within one pulse interval; (3) no silent port hops; (4) no multi-day rogue accumulation; (5) no surviving 0.0.0.0 dev listeners under tailnet_only. Without a harness, these decay into vibes.

REASONING: the stack spans four subsystems (hook, shims, runtime_ops, pulse); only an end-to-end suite proves the LAYERED property — each layer's misses are caught by the next.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: (1) tests/test_port_guard_regression.py: scenario tests wiring real fixture processes (tiny python socket servers masquerading via argv as vite-like signatures where needed) through the shared listener helper, hook script, and pulse classification — each of the five criteria gets at least one test, marked clearly CRITERION-1..5 in test names. (2) A manual proof script scripts/port-guard-proof.sh that runs the same scenarios against the LIVE box with verbose logging and writes a dated report under agent_ergonomics_audit/ or docs/research/ — the operator-facing acceptance artifact. (3) Telemetry rollup: extend pulse-status (and stewardship-report) with cumulative counters {hook_blocks, shim_blocks, post_bind_mismatches, rogues_seen, rogues_reaped, wildcard_criticals} and first/last timestamps; stewardship marks port-guard as assessed once 14 days of counters exist. (4) Document the enforce-flip rule: sentinel default flips to enforce when 14 consecutive days show zero false-positive reports — and record the flip itself as a config change with a dated note.

FILES: tests/test_port_guard_regression.py (new), scripts/port-guard-proof.sh (new), pulse.py/status/stewardship counter plumbing, docs.

RISKS: scenario tests spawning real processes must be hermetic (ephemeral ports, killed in teardown, skipped on unsupported platforms with explicit skip reasons); telemetry counters must survive pulse restarts (persist in pulse.state.json, monotonic).

ACCEPTANCE: (1) five criterion tests exist, named, and pass; (2) proof script produces a dated report with per-criterion PASS/FAIL on a live box; (3) counters persist across pulse restart; (4) stewardship-report shows the port-guard domain with evidence instead of not_assessed; (5) enforce-flip rule documented.

TESTING: this bead IS testing; additionally meta-test that criterion tests fail when a layer is deliberately disabled (mutation-style sanity for at least criterion 2 and 4).

OBSERVABILITY: the telemetry IS the observable; proof reports are dated artifacts.

MAINTAINER NOTES: Future maintainers deciding about eBPF (Phase 5): the answer lives in these counters. If layers 1-4 show leaks the sentinel cannot close, reopen the decision bead — not before.
close_reason: Implemented port-guard criterion regression suite, telemetry rollup, stewardship evidence, and proof script.

title: DECISION: eBPF bind cage (Phase 5) — gated on port-guard telemetry
description:
DECISION (deferred): Whether to build the flag-gated eBPF/cgroup bind cage (BPF_CGROUP_INET4/6_BIND behind SKILLBOX_PORT_CAGE=1) as Phase 5 of the port-guard stack.

BACKGROUND: Codex initially ranked kernel-first enforcement #1 in the 2026-06-11 duel, then conceded "Kernel-first: no. Portable-first: yes" after cross-scoring; consensus moved it to an optional Phase 5 strictly gated on telemetry showing leaks that layers 1-4 cannot close. It is the only mechanism that makes a wrong bind IMPOSSIBLE rather than detected — at the cost of kernel tooling in a deliberately thin, stdlib-first box.

DECISION RULE (agreed in the duel — do not relitigate without new evidence): build ONLY if the port-guard telemetry (see the regression+telemetry bead) shows, over at least 30 days of enforce-mode operation, recurring rogue listeners that survived a full pulse interval or wildcard binds that reached a tailnet-only box. If telemetry stays clean, close this bead as wont-fix with the counters attached.

IF BUILT: scope is a minimal cgroup-attached bind filter for the workspace container allowing only registry-declared ports + ephemeral ranges; loader is optional tooling (not a runtime.yaml-required artifact); failure mode is fail-open with a loud doctor finding (a broken cage must never brick the box); flag-gated, default off, documented as hardened mode.

ACCEPTANCE (for the decision itself): a dated decision note recorded here (and in docs/research/) citing the telemetry window reviewed, with outcome build|wont-fix; if build, spawn fresh implementation beads — do not implement under this bead.

MAINTAINER NOTES: This bead exists so the eBPF idea has exactly one home and cannot be re-pitched fresh each quarter. The duel report (DUELING_WIZARDS_REPORT_PORT_GUARD.md) holds the full scoring history.
close_reason: Operator decision 2026-06-21: drop kernel bind cage permanently; userspace enforcement sufficient. Removed moot dep on 37p9.6.

comments.author: rob-ask-cascade
comments.text: OPERATOR DECISION 2026-06-21 (Rob): DROP the kernel eBPF/cgroup bind cage permanently. Userspace port-guard enforcement (hooks + PATH shims + post-bind verification + rogue-listener sentinel) is sufficient.

title: Pilot network posture proof on portfolio-devbox
description: Pilot the new posture against portfolio-devbox with an operator-safe plan. This Bead should not start by changing live firewall state; first collect current cloud firewall, host firewall/listener, public probe, Tailnet probe, and app exposure evidence, then decide whether any live migration is needed. Model route and authority: Codex gpt-5.5 owns orchestration, system design, implementation, review, commit acceptance, and final say. Claude Opus is only for UX/docs visual review if a docs surface needs design judgment. Grok/Composer 2.5 may run bounded probes only when the validation commands and stronger-model review are explicit. Stop condition: if applying firewall changes could lock out access, require an explicit staged plan and recovery path before mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: evidence artifact proves current portfolio-devbox posture or lists exact drift; if mutation is needed, dry-run, backup/recovery path, and post-change verification are recorded before action. Validation: public TCP probes are from an external/operator vantage point, DO firewall inspection is redacted, Tailnet SSH still works, and no app ports become public.
notes: No-ragrets contract. Outcome: portfolio-devbox proves the lifecycle on a real instance before future boxes depend on it. Evidence: redacted cloud firewall state, host listener/firewall state, external public probes, Tailnet probes, app exposure proof, dry-run and recovery plan before any mutation. Failure avoided: code ships without discovering live-box sequencing or lockout risks.
close_reason: Posture proof collected: box is functionally locked down (SSH key-auth denied, Tailnet works), one drift item (cloud_firewall_missing). Evidence and migration plan in WG-portfolio-devbox-posture-pilot_RESULT.md

description:

Write scope: README/AGENTS/Makefile/runtime docs, client/private-init diagnostics, runtime manager client resolution paths, and focused tests only. Preserve pre-existing dirty `.env-manager/pulse.py` and `tests/test_pulse.py`.

Implementation shape:
- Decide whether the correct behavior is to attach/scaffold `personal`, improve diagnostics for absent private config, or adjust docs/examples to use available clients.

- Keep account/client scope operator-owned; do not fabricate private overlays or secrets.

Non-goals:

close_reason: Made the advertised personal client path truthful and recoverable without fabricating private overlays. Improved runtime_manager/shared.py classify_error: unknown-client errors with no overlays attached now return error_type=unknown_client with an explicit recovery_hint (clients are operator-owned private config; create with client-init/onboard/first-box, or run core-scoped without --client) and actionable next_actions; the 'has no overlay at' focus path returns client_overlay_missing instead of the generic doctor/log fallback. README adds an 'About the personal client' note so examples no longer imply an unexplained client; install.sh --client help clarifies personal is an example slug scaffolded via first-box. description:

Non-goals:
