notes: BLOCKER: superseded by skillbox-cass-rebuild-local-5am-policy-1cok. CASS inline rebuild during agent work is replaced by scheduled local-machine rebuild. This bead is effectively dead — close when policy bead lands.
close_reason: Superseded by CASS scheduled rebuild policy (1cok); inline auto-rebuild no longer planned

title: Safety: close remaining trust-boundary and guard gaps
description:
EPIC: Close the remaining verified trust-boundary and guard gaps so the box's safety story is fail-closed end to end.

Scope: the live findings from the MODES_OF_REASONING report (2026-04-09) and June 2026 re-verification that are STILL open: secret files visible inside the workspace container (K4 remainder), ungated operator_box_exec (S1 remainder), fail-open patterns in guard-destructive-op.sh (S2), dry-run markers not session-scoped and never GC'd, and the EXPECTED_FILES drift class (K2 root cause).

Already fixed — do NOT re-do: dry-run marker path mismatch (both sides now use .skillbox-state/dryrun-markers/ with TTL), env-file injection into workspace services (compose K4 comments), operator_provision marker gating (server-side), pulse MAX_RESTART_ATTEMPTS wiring.

Sequencing: K4 file-visibility fix is the highest-risk design (mount layout change) — isolate it early. Fail-closed guard and box_exec gating land next. Marker session-scoping and EXPECTED_FILES derivation are class-elimination follow-ons.

Exit criteria: an in-container agent cannot read operator credentials; every destructive/remote-exec operator tool is gated; guard scripts exit non-zero on internal errors; doctor proves all of it continuously.
close_reason: All children closed: lzz1.1 (K4), lzz1.2 (S1), lzz1.3 (S2), lzz1.4 (markers), lzz1.5 (K2)

title: K4: move operator secret files out of the workspace bind mount
description:
OBJECTIVE: Make operator secret files unreadable from inside the workspace container, completing the K4 remediation whose env-injection half already landed.

BACKGROUND: docker-compose.yml mounts the repo root read-write at /workspace (line 82: `.:/workspace`, repeated for api/web services). The repo root contains .env, .env.box (DO token, Tailscale authkey) and workspace/boxes.json (fleet inventory). The earlier K4 fix removed env_file injection (see compose comments "K4: operator-scoped .env no longer loaded"), but any in-container agent can still `cat /workspace/.env.box`. MODES K4 rated this 0.95 confidence: credential exfiltration + script rewrite + TOFU redirect in three moves.

REASONING: skillbox's whole trust model is "operator machine controls the fleet; the box is where agents work". Fleet credentials readable by in-box agents collapses that boundary.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: Options (pick via short design note in the PR): (a) move .env/.env.box reads to ${SKILLBOX_STATE_ROOT}/operator/ outside the mounted tree with back-compat fallback + deprecation warning; (b) keep files but switch the workspace mount to an allowlisted subtree set; (c) overlay-exclude via additional tmpfs/empty mounts shadowing the secret paths. (a) is likely cleanest; (c) is the smallest diff but fragile. boxes.json may stay readable if judged non-secret, but document the decision. Update install.sh, Makefile env loading, scripts/box.py, operator_mcp_server.py, 06-upgrade-release.sh preserve-list, and .env.example docs. Provide a migration note (operator moves files once; tooling auto-detects legacy location and warns).

Also add a doctor check (e.g. SECRETS_VISIBLE_IN_WORKSPACE) that fails when known secret filenames are visible under any container-mounted tree, so regressions surface in `make doctor` instead of an audit.

FILES: docker-compose.yml, docker-compose.monoserver.yml, .env.example, install.sh, Makefile, scripts/box.py, scripts/operator_mcp_server.py, scripts/04-reconcile.py (doctor check), scripts/06-upgrade-release.sh, README/docs.

RISKS: breaking every workflow that sources .env from repo root (Makefile does); upgrade script must preserve the new location; remote boxes provisioned by install.sh need the same layout. Edge case: operator intentionally running with no .env.

ACCEPTANCE: (1) `docker compose config` shows no mount that exposes .env/.env.box; (2) from inside a started workspace container, reading the legacy paths fails or returns nothing; (3) make doctor includes the new check and passes on the migrated layout, fails on a planted secret in the mount tree; (4) make render/doctor/runtime-sync/box-status all work with relocated files; (5) migration documented.

TESTING: unit tests for the new env-resolution order (new location, legacy fallback, neither); doctor-check tests with planted fixtures; compose config assertion test (parse YAML, assert no secret-bearing host path). Existing test suites must stay green.

OBSERVABILITY: doctor check result; one-time deprecation warning log when legacy location used.

MAINTAINER NOTES: This is the highest-risk change in the safety epic — do it first and alone in its own PR. Do not silently move files for the operator; print exact mv commands.
close_reason: K4 complete: secrets relocated to ${SKILLBOX_STATE_ROOT}/operator/ with legacy fallback+deprecation warning; new secrets-visible-in-workspace doctor check fails-closed; Makefile --env-file preserves operator overrides; migration is manual (never auto-moved)

title: S1: gate operator_box_exec with command policy + dry-run marker
description:
OBJECTIVE: Gate operator_box_exec with an explicit command policy so remote command execution on fleet boxes is no longer the one ungated escape hatch.

BACKGROUND: guard-destructive-op.sh matches only operator_teardown and operator_compose_down (lines 23-26); operator_provision gained server-side dry-run-marker gating (operator_mcp_server.py lines 779-797). operator_box_exec runs arbitrary commands over Tailscale SSH on any inventory box with no gate at all. MODES S1 (0.95 confidence) flagged this; June 2026 re-verification confirms box_exec is still ungated.

REASONING: teardown is gated but `box_exec "rm -rf /srv/skillbox"` is not — the gate is only as strong as its weakest sibling tool.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: Prefer server-side enforcement in operator_mcp_server.py (works for every client, like the provision gate): (1) classify the command — read-only allowlist (status/journalctl/df/docker ps/cat of non-secret paths/etc.) passes; anything else requires a fresh dry_run=true preview call that returns exactly what would run and stamps a marker (reuse _stamp_dryrun_marker with tool=operator_box_exec, key=box_id+command-hash so a marker for one command cannot authorize another). (2) Optionally integrate dcg (Destructive Command Guard) when installed: pipe the command through `dcg check` and surface its verdict. (3) Always emit an audit event (box_id, command hash, verdict). Add the same matcher to .claude/settings.json hook config for defense-in-depth, but treat server-side as the real gate.

FILES: scripts/operator_mcp_server.py, scripts/guard-destructive-op.sh (matcher additions), .claude/settings.json, tests.

RISKS: over-blocking legitimate ops automation (posture-proof, box status probes shell out via box.py — confirm those paths do not route through the MCP tool); command-hash markers must normalize whitespace to avoid trivial mismatch friction. Edge case: multi-line commands; commands containing secrets (redact in audit log).

ACCEPTANCE: (1) read-only commands run as before; (2) a mutating command without a prior matching dry-run returns a structured rejection with the exact dry-run call to make; (3) marker for command A does not authorize command B; (4) audit events recorded for every box_exec; (5) AGENTS.md/README operator-tool table updated.

TESTING: table-driven unit tests over the classifier (allowlist hits, mutating verbs, hash mismatch, expired marker); MCP-level tests calling the tool handler with mocked subprocess; regression: existing operator MCP tests stay green.

OBSERVABILITY: audit log line per invocation with verdict + reason; rejection payloads carry next_actions.

MAINTAINER NOTES: Keep the allowlist short and boring. The point is friction-with-an-exact-next-step, not prohibition.
close_reason: S1 complete: server-side classify_box_exec_command (conservative read-only allowlist; mutating cmds need fresh dry_run marker bound to box_id+command-hash); per-invocation audit+redaction; guard+settings matcher for defense-in-depth; posture-proof confirmed not routed through box_exec

title: S2: make guard-destructive-op.sh fail closed on internal errors
description:
OBJECTIVE: Make guard-destructive-op.sh fail closed: any internal error in the guard must block the destructive operation, not allow it.

BACKGROUND: The guard currently swallows errors throughout: lines 18-19 (hook input parse `2>/dev/null || echo ""`), 44, 238 (`|| echo "local"`), 244/265/291 (`2>/dev/null || true` around the repo-cleanliness python checks), 366 (marker age read). If python3 is missing, the JSON is malformed, or a repo scan crashes, PROBLEMS comes back empty and the gate passes. MODES S2 (0.90 confidence), re-verified 2026-06-12.

REASONING: a safety gate that passes on its own failure is indistinguishable from no gate the day it matters. The 2026 incident-prevention story (uncommitted work destroyed) depends on these checks actually running.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: (1) set -euo pipefail semantics where compatible with hook protocol; (2) replace each `|| true` / `|| echo default` with explicit branches: on parse failure → exit 2 with a structured "guard could not evaluate, blocking by default" message naming the failed step; (3) distinguish "tool not in scope" (allow) from "evaluation failed" (block); (4) bound the repo scan to the intended roots — verify and fix the scope-inflation noted in MODES (scan walking repo_root/.. ); (5) keep stderr messages actionable: which repo, which check, how to bypass legitimately (commit/push or dry_run). Preserve exit-code contract expected by Claude Code PreToolUse hooks.

FILES: scripts/guard-destructive-op.sh; tests/test_operational_scripts.py (existing shell-harness pattern at lines ~197-293).

RISKS: false-positive blocks if python3 is genuinely absent on a host (acceptable: blocking is the safe failure mode, message explains); flaky SSH during remote repo checks must produce "blocked: could not verify remote" not a hang — add timeouts.

ACCEPTANCE: (1) planted malformed hook input → exit non-zero with explanation; (2) python check raising → blocked, names the step; (3) SSH timeout during remote verification → blocked with timeout reason; (4) clean+pushed repos with valid marker still pass; (5) shellcheck clean.

TESTING: extend the subprocess shell-harness tests: matrix of {good input, malformed JSON, missing python, failing repo script, ssh timeout} x {teardown, compose_down}; assert exit codes and stderr substrings.

OBSERVABILITY: every block prints the failing gate stage; consider a one-line audit append to .skillbox-state/logs (best-effort, never fatal).

MAINTAINER NOTES: When in doubt, block. Operators can always run dry_run=true to re-arm; agents get the exact remediation in stderr.
close_reason: S2 complete: guard fails closed on every internal error of a gated tool (parse/python/repo-scan/ssh), scan scope bounded, ssh timeouts added; non-gated fast path + happy path preserved; shellcheck clean

title: Dry-run markers: session scoping, provenance payload, and GC
description:
OBJECTIVE: Session-scope the dry-run markers and garbage-collect expired ones, so a dry-run by one agent session cannot arm a destructive call from another, and stale markers stop accumulating.

BACKGROUND: Markers live at .skillbox-state/dryrun-markers/.skillbox-dryrun-<tool>-<box>. TTL enforcement exists (operator_mcp_server.py _dryrun_marker_ttl_seconds; guard MARKER_TTL_SECONDS) — the original K1 path mismatch is FIXED, do not redo it. Remaining gaps from MODES K1-secondary: (a) markers are keyed only by tool+box, so any concurrent session inherits another session's arming; (b) expired markers are never deleted, only ignored, so the directory accretes; (c) marker files carry no provenance for audits.

REASONING: the dry-run discipline is the project's signature safety pattern (assess → dry-run → act). Tightening it to session granularity closes the "second agent rides the first agent's marker" hole cheaply.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: (1) include a session discriminator in the marker payload — Claude Code exposes session context to hooks; the MCP server can fall back to parent-PID + start-time when no session id is available; store JSON {tool, key, session, created_at} in the marker instead of relying on mtime alone; (2) verification reads must check session match when a session id is present on both sides, else fall back to current behavior (back-compat); (3) GC: on every marker read/stamp, opportunistically unlink expired markers; document that pulse does NOT own this (markers are operator-side). (4) Update _dryrun_marker_status and the guard's marker read to parse the JSON payload with a legacy-format fallback.

FILES: scripts/operator_mcp_server.py (_dryrun_marker_* family), scripts/guard-destructive-op.sh (marker read ~line 348-390), tests.

RISKS: breaking the existing arm/act flow mid-deprecation — keep reading legacy mtime-only markers for one release, warn when one is consumed; session id absent in some clients (fallback path must be deliberate, not accidental).

ACCEPTANCE: (1) marker stamped in session A does not satisfy session B when both report session ids; (2) expired markers are removed on next gate evaluation; (3) legacy markers still honored with a warning; (4) marker JSON includes provenance readable by `ls` + `cat` for human audit.

TESTING: unit tests over stamp/verify/GC with fabricated sessions and clock control; shell-harness test proving the guard parses both formats.

OBSERVABILITY: rejection payloads state WHY (expired vs session-mismatch vs absent) — distinct reasons, distinct codes.

MAINTAINER NOTES: Related already-closed work fixed the path + TTL; this bead is only scope + hygiene. Keep the fallbacks explicit so the gate never silently weakens.
close_reason: Markers now JSON with session scoping, provenance, GC; guard parses both formats; distinct rejection reasons

title: K2 root fix: derive EXPECTED_FILES from manifests with provenance
description:
OBJECTIVE: Eliminate the EXPECTED_FILES drift class in 04-reconcile.py by making the expected-file list self-validating against the repo's own manifests instead of a hand-maintained constant.

BACKGROUND: scripts/04-reconcile.py line 24 defines a static EXPECTED_FILES list checked by doctor (line 453). MODES K2 (1.0 confidence) caught `make doctor` failing on clean main because a removed helper (skill_bundle_filter.py) stayed in the list. That specific entry is gone today, but the failure CLASS remains: any rename/removal of a listed file, or any new must-exist file that nobody adds, silently rots the doctor's first impression. The README sells doctor as the credibility surface ("confidence that docs/config/runtime still match").

REASONING: a drift detector whose own inputs drift is a standing embarrassment; deriving the list kills the whole class instead of patching instances.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: Not everything is derivable — split EXPECTED_FILES into (a) derived entries: scripts referenced by Makefile targets, compose files referenced by the Makefile composition logic, manifests declared in workspace/ (sandbox/dependencies/persistence/runtime/skill-repos), entrypoints declared in sandbox.yaml; and (b) a small hand-curated core (README.md, AGENTS.md, install.sh) with a comment explaining each. Add a doctor self-check: every static entry must exist AND every derived source must parse; a static entry that has been missing for >0 runs is reported with the exact line to delete. Consider emitting the resolved list under `render` output for transparency.

FILES: scripts/04-reconcile.py, scripts/lib/runtime_model.py (if derivation helpers belong there), tests/test_reconcile.py.

RISKS: over-derivation creating new false failures (e.g. optional compose overlays) — derived entries must respect optionality flags from their source manifests; keep the diff reviewable by printing the before/after lists in the PR.

ACCEPTANCE: (1) deleting a Makefile-referenced script makes doctor fail naming the Makefile target as provenance; (2) removing a file AND its references passes; (3) the static core list is <=10 entries each with a justification comment; (4) clean main stays green.

TESTING: unit tests with fixture trees: missing derived file, missing static file, optional overlay absent, all-present. Regression: full discover suite green.

OBSERVABILITY: doctor output includes provenance per missing-file finding ("expected because Makefile:render").

MAINTAINER NOTES: K2's lesson is that first-run `make doctor` IS the product's handshake. Anything that can make it lie on a clean checkout deserves class-level elimination, not a hotfix.
close_reason: Implemented expected-file provenance hardening and acceptance tests

title: Run SBP skill and MCP visibility recalibration
description:
Run a repo-local `$sbp` recalibration from this repo root before more agent work relies on skill or MCP visibility.

Required read-only pass:
- `sbp`
- `sbp recalibrate`
- `sbp skills --issues-only --no-global --format json`
- `sbp mcp --format json`
- `sbp beads status`

Classify any `missing_for_cwd`, `scope_violations`, `broken_project`, MCP missing/extra/parity drift, and Beads drift. Use narrow dry-runs before applying any sync/remove/prune action. Prefer repo-local fixes; do not do broad global cleanup unless the evidence is specific and recorded here.

concern: Skillbox visibility and MCP parity
writes: project-local skill links, `.mcp.json`, `.codex/config.toml`, repo-local Beads notes only when justified by dry-run evidence
non_goals: broad global pruning; making task skills global to unblock a single run
validation: sbp recalibrate; sbp mcp --format json; sbp beads status
acceptance_criteria:
- SBP read-only commands above were run from the repo root and summarized in issue notes.
- Add/remove/sync decisions are classified by mismatch type and policy reason.
- Any mutation used a reviewed dry-run first, then the narrow apply command only.
- `sbp recalibrate` and `sbp mcp --format json` were rerun after changes, or exact blockers were recorded.
- Remaining human decisions, if any, are explicit.
notes: Backlog refresh 2026-05-11T23:06:37.950786+00:00: confirmed this repo should have this operational bead.
close_reason: SBP skill/MCP recalibration evidence recorded; no narrow mutation needed

Introduced by commit 625251a (still local; repo is 14 commits ahead of origin/main). MUST land before any git push. Add .buildooor/ to .gitignore in the same commit.

title: C2: Pin install.sh SHA256 verification (no skip-on-empty)

Fix: refuse to proceed on empty expected SHA unless explicit --allow-unverified is passed; or pin the default tarball to a tagged release SHA. Also harden the sigstore stub (install.sh:295-306) to not just warn.

global_constraints:

acceptance_criteria: verify_checksum returns non-zero when expected SHA is empty (unless --allow-unverified flag passed); default tarball download path has a pinned SHA OR aborts without explicit override
notes:

Require explicit SKILLBOX_SWIMMERS_EXPOSE=1 AND non-empty SKILLBOX_SWIMMERS_AUTH_TOKEN before rewriting; refuse to start otherwise. H2: --ssh-user and --host flow into f-string {user}@{host} across ~8 call sites (728,756,826,2037,2070,3300) and argparse 3484/3493 with no shape validation. Validate ssh_user against ^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_-]{0,31}$ and --host against DNS/IPv4 at argparse; reject leading dashes; insert -- before destination in every ssh/scp argv.

title: Epic: Durable per-repo skill overrides — committed .skillbox/skill-overrides.yaml, precedence spine, prune firewall, one-question verb contract
description:
Files (where this lands): scripts/sbp (CLI dispatch) -> .env-manager/manage.py -> .env-manager/runtime_manager/{policy_eval, audit_report (collect_skill_visibility:645 / explain_skill_visibility:1635), lifecycle (prune firewall: _plan_skill_prune_actions:589, _unlink:407, guard ~604), inventory, _skill_common (LAYER_* + DISPATCHER_CORE floor), errors, validation (global lint), machines, fleet_converge/relink, agent_snapshots}.py; external policy ../skillbox-config/skill-scope.yaml; NEW .skillbox/skill-overrides.yaml; SKILL files /srv/skillbox/repos/skills-private/{sbp,cass}/SKILL.md; schema scripts/gen_output_schemas.py + docs/SBP_OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.md; tests/{fixture_fleet,test_skill_overrides,test_overlay_regression,test_global_contract,test_output_schema_docs}.py. Each child bead carries its own exact Files: anchor.

Future vantage point: an agent (or operator) can flip any skill on/off for THIS repo, globally, or across a cohort of repos with ONE idempotent command that survives `sbp recalibrate`/sync/prune by construction — and never has to hand-edit the external ../skillbox-config/skill-scope.yaml or fear that a recalibrate 'borks all the settings'.

AUDIENCE: AI agents (primary — they invoke sbp to repair skill visibility: CASS shows 210 missing_for_cwd + 133 recalibrate + 67 activate events across 500 sessions) and the operator (declares durable per-repo defaults, reviewed in git). Bar: I, the agent, would sorely miss a one-command, JSON-emitting, non-clobbering toggle in my next session.

PROBLEM (code is truth): skill visibility = symlinks into .claude/skills + .codex/skills (project) and ~/.claude + ~/.codex (global), derived ONLY from the external sibling repo ../skillbox-config/skill-scope.yaml. `sbp recalibrate` is ALREADY read-only; the 'bork' comes from the mutation it recommends — `sbp skill prune --from project|global --yes` calls _plan_skill_prune_actions (.env-manager/runtime_manager/lifecycle.py:589) which appends _unlink_skill_action at line 604 for anything in issue_keys (scope_violations/global_not_allowed/extra_global/...) with NO pin predicate. A manual toggle IS by definition such a 'violation' against external policy, so it gets deleted. There is no override/pin layer that survives. Globals (14 = DISPATCHER_CORE[smart,sbp] + 12 OPERATOR_EXCEPTIONS, tests/test_global_contract.py:51-66) are unconditional with no per-repo opt-out; overlays are global-only; 'new defaults' require editing the external yaml in two lint-coupled places.

SOLUTION SHAPE: a repo-local committed `.skillbox/skill-overrides.yaml` (pin_on / pin_off / opt_out_global / overlays.enable|disable / defaults / reason) folded as a single override layer into the ONE canonical reader collect_skill_visibility (audit_report.py:645); a precedence spine; a prune firewall (one guard); a one-question verb contract (on/off/activate/heal/why/default); and an agent-friendly fix path that collapses the 3-step ceremony.

SECURITY CONTRACT (must hold): the committed override file is agent-writable, so — local pins may WIDEN visibility within their own repo only; a `--global` pin that contradicts allow_global:false is REFUSED (no privilege escalation); a pin can NEVER disable the global floor (smart, sbp); the schema validator rejects unknown keys; every override is auditable via `sbp skill why`.

SCOPE / RECONCILIATION: extends the no-ragrets weak-surfaces umbrella skillbox-high-use-weak-surfaces-no-ragrets-fxm8 and FOLDS existing beads — skillbox-0eao (recalibrate ceremony) → Phase 4; skillbox-7esf (unclear sbp/cass contract) → Phase 3 SKILL.md contract; skillbox-svx1 (test coupling + gen_output_schemas host-path leak) is a HARD Phase-0 prerequisite (you cannot prove idempotency on a non-deterministic suite). Do not duplicate those beads — depend on / close them.

DONE = Phases 0-3 (P0 critical path) shipped and green: deterministic portable suite, override layer + precedence spine, prune firewall, idempotent on/off + why + floor/escalation refusals + sharpened SKILL.md. Phases 4-7 (P1) layer ergonomics, the generated-surface cascade, defaults/overlay unification, and cross-repo apply. Phase 8 is --defer (journaled transactions, evidence-driven promotion/reaping, portable recipes).

REAL TEST ENTRYPOINT: `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` (Makefile python-cov-xml). Every phase carries unit + e2e test beads with detailed logging. Generated-artifact cascade for any new JSON surface: edit scripts/gen_output_schemas.py → regenerate docs/SBP_OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.md → bump REQUIRED_SURFACES in tests/test_output_schema_docs.py → refresh sbp SKILL.md/AGENTS.md/README.md; write scope = those files only; never embed host paths.

SWARM/LONG-LANE HANDOFF: if implemented by an NTM swarm or headless lane, the observer bead (skill-overrides-swarm-observer) names the polling contract; verify the live pane identity before sending anything; gate check-ins with `sbp send-later ... --when-waiting`.

## Success Criteria
- Phases 0-3 (P0 critical path) are green: a deterministic, machine-portable test suite; the override layer + precedence spine; the prune firewall; idempotent on/off + why + floor/escalation refusals + a sharpened SKILL.md.
- A pin in .skillbox/skill-overrides.yaml SURVIVES sbp recalibrate/sync/prune by construction (the firewall); no manual toggle is ever clobbered.
- The skill-miss ceremony collapses from 3 steps to 1 (skillbox-0eao); the sbp/cass 'unclear contract' cards clear (skillbox-7esf); the suite is deterministic and host-path-free (skillbox-svx1).
- Security contract holds: local pins widen visibility in-repo only; a --global pin contradicting allow_global:false is refused; the floor (smart, sbp) can never be disabled.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests is green; docs/SBP_OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.md regenerates byte-identically on any machine; br dep cycles reports none.
close_reason: Closed the phase-0-through-3 parent gate after durable override spine, prune firewall, idempotent on/off/heal/why, floor/escalation refusals, SKILL contract, recalibrate auto-fix, docs, and full unittest discovery were verified; P1/P2 follow-up beads remain open.

title: Single-source the 14 globals — derive global_allowlist from allow_global rules, regenerate test ALL_GLOBALS from fixture
description:
Files: ../skillbox-config/skill-scope.yaml (global_allowlist + allow_global rules); .env-manager/runtime_manager/validation.py (validate_global_skill_contract, _global_allow_patterns); .env-manager/runtime_manager/_skill_common.py (DISPATCHER_CORE = floor); tests/test_global_contract.py

AUDIENCE: operator (edits policy) + AI agents (trust the global set). Bar: declare a global in ONE place; it cannot drift; the lint becomes a tautology guard, not a hand-sync chore.

BACKGROUND: the always-global surface is declared in THREE coupled places — the top-level global_allowlist list, every allow_global:true rule, and a hardcoded copy in tests/test_global_contract.py (DISPATCHER_CORE=[smart,sbp] + OPERATOR_EXCEPTIONS=12 → ALL_GLOBALS=14, lines 51-66). validate_global_skill_contract babysits the first two; the test pastes the third. The TRUTH is 14; the live external skill-scope.yaml drifts in a 15th (make-indispensable) — that drift IS bead skillbox-svx1.

WHAT: make the allow_global:true rules the single source of truth; compute global_allowlist as a DERIVED projection at load (the union _global_allow_patterns already computes); keep an OPTIONAL hand-authored allowlist only as an asserted snapshot the lint checks against the derived union (readability, back-compat). Regenerate the test's expected global set from the FIXTURE policy, not a pasted constant, so it cannot independently drift. Floor constant for later phases = DISPATCHER_CORE (reused, never a new list).

WHY/CONSIDERATIONS: the dual/triple declaration is a latent clobber/leak source and the reason svx1's lint is non-portable. Single-source kills the class. Must FIRST prove current parity (capture both declarations, diff) before collapsing, or live visibility could change.

DONE: removing a skill from the rules removes it from the derived allowlist with no second edit; hand-adding to the static allowlist fails the lint with 'this list is derived; edit rules instead'; the existing 14-skill effective set is unchanged (golden parity before/after); test ALL_GLOBALS is loaded from the fixture. TEST: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests (test_global_contract.py) green on a box with NO ../skillbox-config sibling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Removing a skill from the allow_global rules drops it from the derived global_allowlist with NO second edit; hand-adding to the static allowlist fails the lint with 'this list is derived; edit rules instead'.
- The test's expected global set is loaded from the fixture policy (not a pasted constant); the existing 14-skill effective set is unchanged (golden parity before/after).
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests green on a box with NO ../skillbox-config sibling.
acceptance_criteria:
- Removing a skill from the allow_global rules drops it from the derived global_allowlist with NO second edit; hand-adding to the static allowlist fails the lint with 'this list is derived; edit rules instead'.
- The test's expected global set is loaded from the fixture policy (not a pasted constant); the existing 14-skill effective set is unchanged (golden parity before/after).
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests green on a box with NO ../skillbox-config sibling.
close_reason: Implemented allow_global rules as the single source for global skill grants: runtime ignores snapshot-only global_allowlist grants, validation treats global_allowlist as an optional derived snapshot, DISPATCHER_CORE is shared for later floor work, and tests cover snapshot/no-snapshot/runtime behavior. Verified focused tests (61 tests OK) and full python3 -m unittest discover -s tests (1700 tests, skipped=4). Committed as 5135ab2.

title: Phase 2 tests — property-test the firewall over random (policy, override) inputs, plan==apply
description:
Files: .env-manager/runtime_manager/lifecycle.py; tests/test_skill_overrides.py / tests/test_overlay_regression.py (seeded property test); tests/fixture_fleet.py

AUDIENCE: AI agents (test-trust) + operator. Bar: the firewall is proven over GENERATED inputs, not three hand-picked fixtures, and cannot leak host paths.

WHAT: a seeded property test over fixture_fleet drawing (policy, override) pairs from a BOUNDED, fixture-relative alphabet of skills/repos; assert invariants: (a) no pin_on skill ever appears in a prune unlink plan; (b) sync→prune→sync reaches a fixed point (third op is a no-op); (c) the effective set never contains a pin_off skill; (d) every output path is under the fixture root (no $HOME, no /srv leakage). Plus the byte-equal dry-run==apply assertion from the firewall bead.

DONE: N seeded cases all hold; a guard assertion fails loudly if any output contains the real $HOME/external-config path; the bounded alphabet is enforced. TEST: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on the firewall.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A seeded property test over a bounded, fixture-relative alphabet holds: no pin_on skill ever appears in a prune unlink plan; sync->prune->sync reaches a fixed point; the effective set never contains a pin_off skill.
- A guard fails loudly if any output path leaks $HOME or the external-config path.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- A seeded property test over a bounded, fixture-relative alphabet holds: no pin_on skill ever appears in a prune unlink plan; sync->prune->sync reaches a fixed point; the effective set never contains a pin_off skill.
- A guard fails loudly if any output path leaks $HOME or the external-config path.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.

title: sbp skill on/off — idempotent durable pin (on returns the activation packet), --dry-run, --verify
description:
Files: scripts/sbp (skill dispatch ~1050-1057); .env-manager/manage.py (skill subcommand); .env-manager/runtime_manager/lifecycle.py (_activations_from_sync_plan, skill_lifecycle_plan); /srv/skillbox/repos/skills-private/sbp/SKILL.md (install: home/.claude/skills/sbp/SKILL.md); tests/test_skill_overrides.py

AUDIENCE: AI agents (primary). Bar: I run `sbp skill on wiki` once; it pins durably AND hands back the SKILL.md so I use it immediately, and running it twice is a clean no-op (exit 0, identical JSON) so I can put it in a retry loop without fear.

VERB SEMANTICS (resolved — this is the 7esf 'unclear contract' fix): on = writes pin_on to the repo override file (durable, survives recalibrate) + links disk + RETURNS the activation packet {name, source, skill_md_path, skill_md_sha256, skill_md}; off = writes pin_off (durable) + unlinks (firewall-safe). on/off share activate's code path (_activations_from_sync_plan) — on == activate + a policy write; do NOT let them diverge. The agent's whole decision collapses to one question: 'do I want this to stick?' yes→on, no→activate. Scope: repo by default, --global writes to the global layer (subject to the escalation refusal bead).

IDEMPOTENCY: read merged state → if writing would not change the merged eval, emit {changed:false, noop:true}, touch NOTHING, exit 0; else write atomically + emit the relink plan. Reuse the EXACT merge function (no reimplementation) so noop:true never lies. Support --dry-run (preview the would_link/would_pin without mutating; symmetric with the CLI's dry-run-first safety culture) and --verify (re-resolve visibility post-link and return {verified, effective_now, symlink_resolved, skill_md_sha256} where the packet sha256 matches the linked target's SKILL.md — read the disk/symlink, not a pane-buffered view, per the lag memory).

DONE: on X then on X → second run actions==[], exit 0, byte-identical JSON (timestamp omitted/pinned); on X then off X round-trips the file bytes; on X --verify shows verified:true and effective_now contains X; on X --dry-run makes zero filesystem change. TEST: tests/test_skill_overrides.py; python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on the spine and the firewall.

## Acceptance Criteria
- on X then on X: the second run has actions==[], exit 0, and byte-identical JSON (timestamp omitted/pinned); on X then off X round-trips the file bytes.
- on returns the activation packet; on X --verify reports verified:true with effective_now containing X; on X --dry-run makes zero filesystem changes.
- on/off reuse activate's plan code path (no divergence). Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- on X then on X: the second run has actions==[], exit 0, and byte-identical JSON (timestamp omitted/pinned); on X then off X round-trips the file bytes.
- on returns the activation packet; on X --verify reports verified:true with effective_now containing X; on X --dry-run makes zero filesystem changes.
- on/off reuse activate's plan code path (no divergence). Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: Implemented durable skill on/off commands with idempotent activation packets, dry-run, verify, registry coverage, and full unittest verification.

title: Global floor veto + global-escalation refusal (security boundary for the agent-writable file)
description:
Files: .env-manager/runtime_manager/policy_eval.py (_global_install_allowed); .env-manager/runtime_manager/_skill_common.py (DISPATCHER_CORE floor); .env-manager/runtime_manager/errors.py; tests/test_global_contract.py, tests/test_skill_overrides.py

AUDIENCE: operator + AI agents. Bar: the committed override file can widen visibility within its own repo but can NEVER disable the router or escalate to global — privilege escalation is structurally blocked.

WHAT: enforce three rules in the on/off/default path — (1) pin_off of a global-FLOOR skill (DISPATCHER_CORE = smart, sbp) is REFUSED with error OVERRIDE_REFUSED_FLOOR (you cannot brick the tool that turns things back on); (2) a --global pin that contradicts a rule's allow_global:false is REFUSED with OVERRIDE_REFUSED_GLOBAL_ESCALATION (a --global pin against an explicit non-global decision is escalation across all repos); (3) a LOCAL repo pin is ALWAYS allowed (blast radius = one reviewable checkout). Floor constant = DISPATCHER_CORE (reused from the single-source-globals bead — never a new list). The 12 OPERATOR_EXCEPTIONS are global-by-allowlist but NOT floor: a repo may legitimately opt_out_global mmdx/git-stash-janitor locally.

WHY: the override file is agent-writable; this is its security contract. DONE: off sbp → non-zero exit + OVERRIDE_REFUSED_FLOOR, file unchanged; on X --global where X is allow_global:false → OVERRIDE_REFUSED_GLOBAL_ESCALATION; local pin_on of a default-off skill → allowed, INFO. TEST: tests/test_skill_overrides.py + extend tests/test_global_contract.py with the floor/default split; python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on on/off and single-source-globals.

## Acceptance Criteria
- off sbp returns non-zero with OVERRIDE_REFUSED_FLOOR and leaves the file unchanged; on X --global where X is allow_global:false returns OVERRIDE_REFUSED_GLOBAL_ESCALATION; a local pin_on of a default-off skill is allowed (INFO).
- The floor constant is DISPATCHER_CORE (reused, not a new list).
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests (extends tests/test_global_contract.py).
acceptance_criteria:
- off sbp returns non-zero with OVERRIDE_REFUSED_FLOOR and leaves the file unchanged; on X --global where X is allow_global:false returns OVERRIDE_REFUSED_GLOBAL_ESCALATION; a local pin_on of a default-off skill is allowed (INFO).
- The floor constant is DISPATCHER_CORE (reused, not a new list).
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests (extends tests/test_global_contract.py).
close_reason: Implemented dispatcher floor and global-escalation refusal guards for skill on/off, with typed override errors, --global parser alias, policy context, focused tests, and full unittest verification.

title: Override error taxonomy + exit-code contract (codes, not new classes)
description:
Files: .env-manager/runtime_manager/errors.py (add codes to the stable 'code' taxonomy + codes-enumeration snapshot test); tests/

AUDIENCE: AI agents. Bar: a refusal or a malformed file yields a stable, machine-branchable code and the right exit status, so the agent recovers instead of improvising (the 7esf failure mode).

BACKGROUND: .env-manager/runtime_manager/errors.py uses a deliberately shallow taxonomy where new distinctions go in the stable string 'code', NOT new subclasses (error.code == error.type == top-level error_code by contract).

WHAT: add codes OVERRIDE_REFUSED_FLOOR, OVERRIDE_REFUSED_GLOBAL_ESCALATION, OVERRIDE_PARSE_ERROR (malformed yaml; fail safe to base policy), OVERRIDE_SKILL_UNKNOWN (with did-you-mean), and PRUNE_SKIPPED_PINNED (info, exit 0). Exit-code contract: idempotent no-ops and info → exit 0; refusals and unknown-skill → non-zero. Wire into the snapshot test that enumerates known codes (errors.py docstring references it).

DONE: each code is raised in its scenario with the agreed exit status and appears in the codes snapshot test. TEST: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on the schema reader.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each code (OVERRIDE_REFUSED_FLOOR, OVERRIDE_REFUSED_GLOBAL_ESCALATION, OVERRIDE_PARSE_ERROR, OVERRIDE_SKILL_UNKNOWN, PRUNE_SKIPPED_PINNED) is raised in its scenario with the agreed exit status and appears in the codes-enumeration snapshot test.
- Idempotent no-ops and info exit 0; refusals and unknown-skill exit non-zero. Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- Each code (OVERRIDE_REFUSED_FLOOR, OVERRIDE_REFUSED_GLOBAL_ESCALATION, OVERRIDE_PARSE_ERROR, OVERRIDE_SKILL_UNKNOWN, PRUNE_SKIPPED_PINNED) is raised in its scenario with the agreed exit status and appears in the codes-enumeration snapshot test.
- Idempotent no-ops and info exit 0; refusals and unknown-skill exit non-zero. Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: Implemented stable override/prune error codes, exit-status contract, pinned-prune info handling, and code snapshot tests. Verified: python3 -m unittest tests.test_errors tests.test_skill_overrides tests.test_skill_visibility tests.test_cli_units; python3 -m unittest discover -s tests (1711 tests OK, skipped=4).

title: sbp skill why X — full provenance trace that ALSO explains absence, with the exact fix command
description:
Files: .env-manager/runtime_manager/audit_report.py (explain_skill_visibility:1635 — extend to absence + fix command); scripts/sbp (skill why dispatch); tests/test_skill_overrides.py, tests/goldens/

AUDIENCE: AI agents debugging a visibility miss (the exact 'I can't see wiki' memory trigger). Bar: one command prints every layer that touched skill X, what each said, which won — and if X is NOT effective here, WHY and the literal command to fix it. This is the 0eao 3-step→1-step collapse on the read side.

WHAT: extend explain_skill_visibility (audit_report.py:1635) to walk the precedence spine and emit an ordered layers[] trace with a single wins:true layer == winning_layer. CRITICAL: `why` must explain ABSENCE, not just presence — if X is off, say which case (no rule matches this cwd / default-off / pinned_off / opt_out_global / broken link) AND print the exact `sbp skill on X --cwd $PWD` remediation. Drive `why` from the SAME merge function the effective set uses (never a parallel reimplementation, or it lies). --json and text.

DONE: golden traces for a pinned-on local skill, an opt_out_global, an overlay-gated-off skill, a floor skill ignoring a pin_off, and an ABSENT skill (with fix command). winning_layer always equals the wins:true layer; goldens are fixture-relative (no host paths). TEST: tests/test_skill_overrides.py + tests/goldens; python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on the precedence spine.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Golden traces exist for: a pinned-on local skill, an opt_out_global, an overlay-gated-off skill, a floor skill ignoring a pin_off, and an ABSENT skill (with the exact 'sbp skill on X --cwd $PWD' fix).
- winning_layer always equals the wins:true layer; why is driven by the same merge function as the effective set; goldens are fixture-relative.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- Golden traces exist for: a pinned-on local skill, an opt_out_global, an overlay-gated-off skill, a floor skill ignoring a pin_off, and an ABSENT skill (with the exact 'sbp skill on X --cwd $PWD' fix).
- winning_layer always equals the wins:true layer; why is driven by the same merge function as the effective set; goldens are fixture-relative.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: Implemented sbp skill why and extended explain_skill_visibility with canonical winning_layer/layers trace, durable skill-on remediation, override provenance, floor-veto trace rows, and fixture-local golden coverage for pinned-on, opt-out, overlay-gated, floor-veto, and absent/source-backed cases. Verified focused pytest/unittest suites and full python3 -m unittest discover -s tests (1751 tests OK, skipped=4).

title: Rewrite sbp SKILL.md verb contract (+ tighten cass) — non-goals, do-NOT, one-question decision tree (closes 7esf)
description:
Files: /srv/skillbox/repos/skills-private/sbp/SKILL.md (install: home/.claude/skills/sbp/SKILL.md) and /srv/skillbox/repos/skills-private/cass/SKILL.md (the skill files — verb-contract table, do-NOT, one-question tree); mirror the verbs implemented in scripts/sbp + .env-manager/runtime_manager/lifecycle.py

AUDIENCE: AI agents picking a verb. Bar: a single machine-and-human disambiguation so I pick the right verb first try; closes the lube 'unclear skill contract' card (sbp scored 119/28-runs, cass 111/15-runs).

WHAT: rewrite the sbp SKILL.md with a verb-contract TABLE — for each of {recalibrate, activate, sync, prune, on, off, default, heal, why}: purpose, mutates (none|cwd-ephemeral|repo-state|disk-links), links disk?, returns_packet?, scope, survives_recalibrate?. Add an explicit 'do NOT' section: do not pin_off dispatcher-core (smart/sbp); do not --global-pin to escalate visibility; activate is EPHEMERAL — use `on` to persist; recalibrate is read-only — it recommends, it does not mutate. Add the one-question decision tree ('do I want this to stick?'). Per 7esf, also tighten the cass SKILL.md (search/query tool, not a file reader; auto-rebuild behavior; non-goals).

DONE: both SKILL.md files carry non-goals + do-NOT + branch rules; a re-score would drop the 'unclear contract' card. This is also mirrored as machine-readable data in the verb-contract-json bead. ## Acceptance Criteria
- The sbp SKILL.md carries the verb-contract table (purpose/mutates/links/returns_packet/scope/survives_recalibrate) for recalibrate|activate|sync|prune|on|off|default|heal|why, plus an explicit do-NOT section and the one-question decision tree.
- The cass SKILL.md is tightened (search/query tool not a file reader; auto-rebuild behavior; non-goals).

title: Phase 3 tests — the six idempotency/precedence/floor/portability invariants
description:
Files: tests/test_skill_overrides.py (the six invariants)

AUDIENCE: implementing agent. Bar: the core verbs are proven deterministic and portable.

WHAT: in tests/test_skill_overrides.py assert the six invariants: (1) on(X);on(X) → second actions==[], exit 0, byte-identical JSON; same for off; (2) with a pin on X, prune dry-run plan == apply plan byte-equal and neither unlinks X (skipped:pinned); (3) absent .skillbox/ → effective set == base policy; (4) file pin_on:X + base off:X → why reports repo-override-file winning by LAYER NAME; (5) off sbp → non-zero + OVERRIDE_REFUSED_FLOOR, file unchanged; (6) whole suite green with SKILLBOX_CLIENTS_HOST_ROOT=$tmp and NO skillbox-config sibling, with a guard that no output contains $HOME/external paths.

title: sbp skill heal X — resolve source + pin + link + activation packet in ONE idempotent command
description:
Files: scripts/sbp (skill heal dispatch); .env-manager/runtime_manager/lifecycle.py (activation packet) + .env-manager/runtime_manager/inventory.py (source resolution); .skillbox/skill-overrides.yaml (NEW, repo-committed); /srv/skillbox/repos/skills-private/sbp/SKILL.md (install: home/.claude/skills/sbp/SKILL.md); tests/test_skill_overrides.py

AUDIENCE: the agent mid-task that says 'I don't see wiki, I'll fall back to git history' — the exact anti-pattern sbp exists to prevent. Bar: `sbp skill heal wiki` makes the miss self-correcting, durable, AND instantly usable in one move.

WHAT: heal = detect → resolve source (inventory) → write pin_on to the repo override file → link → emit the activation packet — as one idempotent op (healing an already-visible skill is a no-op that still returns the packet). REFUSE if no source resolves (OVERRIDE_SKILL_UNKNOWN with did-you-mean) rather than write a dangling pin. heal == on + source-auto-resolution; share the on/off code path.

WHY: collapses the documented 3-step ceremony (recalibrate → missing_for_cwd → activate → retry; 210/133/67 events) to ONE command whose own output hands back the capability. CONSIDERATION: tag healed pins with reason:heal + timestamp so the linter/reaper can later question them.

DONE: in a fixture where wiki is policy-blocked for the cwd, heal wiki produces a pinned override + live symlink + packet whose skill_md_sha256 matches the source; a second invocation is a no-op + same packet; heal of an unresolvable skill refuses without writing. TEST: tests/test_skill_overrides.py; python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on on/off and the error taxonomy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- heal X on a policy-blocked cwd produces a pinned override + live symlink + activation packet whose skill_md_sha256 matches the source; a second invocation is a no-op that still returns the packet.
- heal of an unresolvable skill refuses with OVERRIDE_SKILL_UNKNOWN (did-you-mean) without writing a dangling pin; healed pins are tagged reason:heal.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- heal X on a policy-blocked cwd produces a pinned override + live symlink + activation packet whose skill_md_sha256 matches the source; a second invocation is a no-op that still returns the packet.
- heal of an unresolvable skill refuses with OVERRIDE_SKILL_UNKNOWN (did-you-mean) without writing a dangling pin; healed pins are tagged reason:heal.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: Implemented sbp skill heal with source validation, durable pin/link activation packet, strict verify, docs, and tests.

title: Machine-readable skill_verbs map in capabilities --json + robot-docs
description:
Files: scripts/sbp (print_capabilities ~216-283, robot-docs ~286-316); /srv/skillbox/repos/skills-private/sbp/SKILL.md (install: home/.claude/skills/sbp/SKILL.md); docs/SBP_OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.md; tests/

AUDIENCE: AI agents choosing a verb programmatically. Bar: a queryable disambiguation map so I never reach for the wrong verb.

WHAT: add a skill_verbs object to `sbp capabilities --json` (and mirror in robot-docs guide) — for each of {recalibrate, activate, sync, prune, on, off, default, heal, why}: {purpose, mutates, links_disk, returns_packet, scope, survives_recalibrate, when_to_use, do_NOT}. This is the machine mirror of the SKILL.md verb table.

DONE: capabilities --json contains skill_verbs with every dispatched skill subcommand; a test asserts on.returns_packet==true, recalibrate.mutates=='none', activate.mutates=='cwd-ephemeral', and that EVERY dispatched skill subcommand has an entry (no orphans). Mark CONTRACT in the schema doc. TEST: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on the SKILL.md contract bead.

## Acceptance Criteria
- sbp capabilities --json contains a skill_verbs entry for EVERY dispatched skill subcommand (no orphans); a test asserts on.returns_packet==true, recalibrate.mutates=='none', activate.mutates=='cwd-ephemeral'.
- Fields are marked CONTRACT in the schema doc. Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- sbp capabilities --json contains a skill_verbs entry for EVERY dispatched skill subcommand (no orphans); a test asserts on.returns_packet==true, recalibrate.mutates=='none', activate.mutates=='cwd-ephemeral'.
- Fields are marked CONTRACT in the schema doc. Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: Added skill_verbs to sbp capabilities, mirrored it in robot-docs, generated schema docs, and verified full suite.

title: recalibrate --auto-fix + failure-path fold — at most 1 sbp command per visibility miss
description:
Files: scripts/sbp (recalibrate action lines 884-965); .env-manager/runtime_manager/lifecycle.py (heal path); tests/

AUDIENCE: AI agents hitting a visibility miss. Bar: the recovery is one command or one structured payload, never the 3-step ceremony.

WHAT: add `sbp recalibrate --auto-fix` that auto-runs heal for each missing_for_cwd skill (dry-run by default, --yes to apply) and reports what it activated; and ensure the 'skill not loaded' recovery surfaces the exact `sbp skill on <skill> --cwd $PWD` (a sbp-side suggester so the message and the CLI never drift). This is bead skillbox-0eao's option B (durable) built on heal.

DONE: on a cwd with N missing_for_cwd skills, recalibrate --auto-fix --yes activates them and prints the activation packets; --auto-fix (no --yes) previews exactly what it would link. TEST: e2e bead below + python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on heal.

## Acceptance Criteria
- recalibrate --auto-fix --yes activates each missing_for_cwd skill and prints the activation packets; recalibrate --auto-fix (no --yes) previews exactly what it would link.
- The recovery surface emits the exact 'sbp skill on <skill> --cwd $PWD' via a single sbp-side suggester so message and CLI never drift.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- recalibrate --auto-fix --yes activates each missing_for_cwd skill and prints the activation packets; recalibrate --auto-fix (no --yes) previews exactly what it would link.
- The recovery surface emits the exact 'sbp skill on <skill> --cwd $PWD' via a single sbp-side suggester so message and CLI never drift.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: Added recalibrate --auto-fix heal preview/apply flow, durable missing-skill recovery text, docs, and tests.

title: Swarm/long-lane observer & handoff contract for the overrides epic
description:
Files: scripts/sbp_send_later.sh (sbp send-later --when-waiting check-in); the epic's per-phase test commands; expected artifacts: tests/test_skill_overrides.py, docs/SBP_OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.md, .skillbox/skill-overrides.yaml (NEW, repo-committed)

AUDIENCE: the supervising agent/operator if this epic runs through an NTM swarm or headless lane.

WHAT: define the observer contract for long-lane implementation — (1) evidence of the live lane: the tmux/NTM target pane id and its `br` actor; (2) expected artifacts per phase (new test files green, docs/SBP_OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.md regenerated, .skillbox/skill-overrides.yaml fixture present); (3) close marker: `br` issue moves to closed with the phase's test command output pasted in a comment; (4) first safe polling window: 20-30 min after launch (cache-friendly), not sooner; (5) no-nudge conditions: lane is mid-edit or tests running; (6) supervisor timer: `sbp send-later new --when-waiting ...` gated check-in that VERIFIES the live pane identity before sending anything (see memory: pane-written files lag foreground reads — confirm via the pane's own robot state, not a foreground file read).

WHY: prevents premature nudges and lost work across a multi-session implementation. DONE: this contract is referenced by the implementing swarm's marching orders; no check-in is sent without prior pane-identity verification.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The observer contract is documented: live-pane id + br actor, per-phase expected artifacts, close marker, first safe poll window (20-30 min), no-nudge conditions, and the sbp send-later --when-waiting timer.
- It is referenced by the implementing swarm's marching orders.
- No check-in is sent without prior live-pane-identity verification (per the pane-visibility-lag memory).
acceptance_criteria:
- The observer contract is documented: live-pane id + br actor, per-phase expected artifacts, close marker, first safe poll window (20-30 min), no-nudge conditions, and the sbp send-later --when-waiting timer.
- It is referenced by the implementing swarm's marching orders.
- No check-in is sent without prior live-pane-identity verification (per the pane-visibility-lag memory).
close_reason: Documented the long-lane observer contract and added a skill-overrides swarm WORKGRAPH reference plus regression test. Verified: python3 -m unittest tests.test_skill_overrides_swarm_contract (2 tests OK).

title: E2E: skill-miss ceremony collapses to one command (closes 0eao)
description:
Files: scripts/sbp (recalibrate / skill heal); .env-manager/runtime_manager/evidence.py (sbp evidence — measure ceremony); tests/test_skill_overrides.py or a tests/e2e script

AUDIENCE: AI agents + the operator measuring ceremony reduction. Bar: future sessions show at most 1 sbp command per visibility miss instead of 3.

WHAT: an e2e test script with detailed logging that simulates the canonical failure — invoke a skill not visible for the cwd → run the single recovery command (heal or recalibrate --auto-fix) → assert the skill is now effective AND the activation packet was returned in the SAME command, with NO separate recalibrate+activate calls. Log each step and the tool-call count so post-implementation verification is unambiguous.

DONE: the e2e asserts ceremony==1 command; pairs with the CASS evidence join (`sbp evidence`) to confirm fewer missing_for_cwd→recalibrate→activate chains in real sessions. Closes skillbox-0eao. TEST: a tests/e2e script + python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on heal and recalibrate --auto-fix.

## Acceptance Criteria
- An e2e script asserts the skill-miss ceremony collapses to ONE command: invoke an unavailable skill, run the single recovery command, and the skill is effective AND the activation packet was returned in the same call, with NO separate recalibrate+activate.
- The tool-call count is logged. Closes skillbox-0eao. Verify: the e2e script + python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- An e2e script asserts the skill-miss ceremony collapses to ONE command: invoke an unavailable skill, run the single recovery command, and the skill is effective AND the activation packet was returned in the same call, with NO separate recalibrate+activate.
- The tool-call count is logged. Closes skillbox-0eao. Verify: the e2e script + python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: Added a real wrapper-level E2E proving one outer sbp recalibrate --auto-fix --yes command repairs a temp missing_for_cwd skill, returns the embedded heal activation packet, writes repo override/link state, and clears the missing skill. Verified python3 -m unittest discover -s tests: 1777 tests, OK, skipped=4.

title: recalibrate --json with machine-actionable fixes[] (flip json:false -> true)
description:
Files: scripts/sbp (recalibrate action 884-965; capabilities json:false->true ~line 251); scripts/gen_output_schemas.py; tests/test_output_schema_docs.py

AUDIENCE: agents that land on recalibrate (133/500 sessions) and must eyeball a human dump. Bar: I parse fixes[0].command and run it, never reading prose.

BACKGROUND: recalibrate is the one surface marked json:false (scripts/sbp:251 / capabilities). It already COMPUTES the dry-run fixes; it just doesn't emit them as data.

WHAT: add `sbp recalibrate --json` emitting {fixes:[{problem, skill, command, links:[...], dry_run_preview, packet_on_apply}]} where each command is literal and copy-pasteable; flip json:false→true. Mark fixes[].command and fixes[].skill as CONTRACT.

DONE: recalibrate --json validates against the new locked schema entry; each fixes[].command executes verbatim and clears its issue. TEST: tests/test_output_schema_docs.py (see the cascade bead) + python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on recalibrate --auto-fix.

## Acceptance Criteria
- sbp recalibrate --json emits fixes[] with a literal copy-pasteable command per issue; the recalibrate surface flips json:false -> true.
- Each fixes[].command executes verbatim and clears its issue; the output validates against the locked schema entry (CONTRACT on command + skill).
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- sbp recalibrate --json emits fixes[] with a literal copy-pasteable command per issue; the recalibrate surface flips json:false -> true.
- Each fixes[].command executes verbatim and clears its issue; the output validates against the locked schema entry (CONTRACT on command + skill).
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: Implemented sbp recalibrate --json as a parseable machine payload with fixes[] commands, dry-run previews, capabilities json:true, regenerated schema docs, and wrapper/schema/full-suite validation.

title: Generated-artifact cascade — register new JSON surfaces, regenerate SBP_OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.md, bump REQUIRED_SURFACES
description:
Files: scripts/gen_output_schemas.py (SURFACES list ~421, FIELD_NOTES, example_*); docs/SBP_OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.md (regen; write scope = this file only); tests/test_output_schema_docs.py (REQUIRED_SURFACES 7->N)

AUDIENCE: AI agents parsing sbp JSON. Bar: every new surface is a stable, locked, host-path-free machine contract.

GENERATED-ARTIFACT CASCADE (producers → consumers → refresh order → write scope): (1) add example payload + SURFACES entry in scripts/gen_output_schemas.py for each new surface — recalibrate(--fixes), skill why, skill on, skill off, skill togglable; (2) regenerate docs/SBP_OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.md via the generator (write scope = ONLY that file); (3) bump REQUIRED_SURFACES + rename test_all_seven_surfaces_are_documented in tests/test_output_schema_docs.py (7 → N); (4) add FIELD_NOTES with CONTRACT/info stability per field. PREREQUISITE: the generator must be path-redacted/deterministic FIRST (bead skillbox-svx1 #2 — it currently leaks real repo paths like /srv/.../meaning); fix that before adding surfaces or you bake the non-portability into N surfaces instead of 1.

DONE: the committed doc matches the generator on any machine; test_committed_doc_matches_generator + test_generator_is_deterministic + the surface-count test are green; no host paths in the doc. TEST: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests (REGEN_OUTPUT_SCHEMA_DOCS=1 to regenerate). Depends on recalibrate --fixes, why, on/off, and svx1.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The new surfaces (recalibrate --fixes, skill why, skill on, skill off, skill togglable) are added to scripts/gen_output_schemas.py; docs/SBP_OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.md is regenerated (write scope = that file only); REQUIRED_SURFACES is bumped 7->N; FIELD_NOTES carry CONTRACT/info per field.
- The generator is path-redacted first; test_committed_doc_matches_generator and test_generator_is_deterministic are green on any machine.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- The new surfaces (recalibrate --fixes, skill why, skill on, skill off, skill togglable) are added to scripts/gen_output_schemas.py; docs/SBP_OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.md is regenerated (write scope = that file only); REQUIRED_SURFACES is bumped 7->N; FIELD_NOTES carry CONTRACT/info per field.
- The generator is path-redacted first; test_committed_doc_matches_generator and test_generator_is_deterministic are green on any machine.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: Implemented sbp skill togglable and schema docs

title: sbp skill default on/off X --repo|--global — set new defaults without yaml surgery (dry-run==apply)
description:
Files: scripts/sbp (skill default dispatch); .env-manager/runtime_manager/policy_eval.py + .env-manager/runtime_manager/validation.py (--global single-source); .skillbox/skill-overrides.yaml (NEW, repo-committed); ../skillbox-config/skill-scope.yaml; tests/test_skill_overrides.py

AUDIENCE: operator + agents setting defaults. Bar: 'make a new default' is a first-class verb, scope-explicit and reviewable, not hand-editing an external yaml in two coupled places.

WHAT: `sbp skill default on X --repo` upserts the defaults/pin_on block in .skillbox/skill-overrides.yaml; `--global` adds an allow_global rule in the external policy in ONE edit (via the single-source-globals path, keeping validate_global_skill_contract green); --dry-run prints the unified diff and writes nothing; apply produces the IDENTICAL change (dry-run==apply). Use a round-trip-preserving YAML writer (don't clobber comments/order). --global writes outside the repo, so honor the commit/dry-run safety gate.

DONE: default on X --repo --dry-run prints a diff and writes nothing; the same without --dry-run yields a file whose re-parse makes X effective at that cwd; --global edit keeps the global contract lint green. TEST: tests/test_skill_overrides.py; python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on on/off and single-source-globals.

## Acceptance Criteria
- default on X --repo --dry-run prints a unified diff and writes nothing; the same without --dry-run yields a file whose re-parse makes X effective at that cwd.
- A --global edit keeps validate_global_skill_contract green; the YAML writer preserves comments/order.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- default on X --repo --dry-run prints a unified diff and writes nothing; the same without --dry-run yields a file whose re-parse makes X effective at that cwd.
- A --global edit keeps validate_global_skill_contract green; the YAML writer preserves comments/order.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: Implemented sbp skill default on/off for repo/global defaults with dry-run/apply diffs, effective repo pins, comment-preserving targeted YAML edits, global --yes gate, global contract validation, wrapper/registry/docs updates, and full unittest discovery.

title: sbp skill togglable --cwd --json — one-read switchboard of everything flippable here
description:
Files: .env-manager/runtime_manager/audit_report.py (write-affordance view); scripts/sbp (skill togglable); docs/SBP_OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.md; tests/test_skill_overrides.py AUDIENCE: an agent orienting in a new repo. Bar: one read tells me every skill I can flip here, its state, and the command to flip it — no cross-referencing skills+candidates+policy. WHAT: `sbp skill togglable --cwd

--json` → {cwd, items:[{skill, state: on|off|missing_for_cwd|pinned_on|pinned_off, source, pinned_by: override|policy, command_to_flip}]}. This is the WRITE-affordance view (what is flippable), distinct from `skills` (what is visible). DONE: in the fixture estate, togglable --json lists every skill with a deterministic state and a command_to_flip that, when executed, transitions the state as advertised; golden-locked, fixture-relative. TEST: tests/test_skill_overrides.py + the schema cascade; python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on why. ## Acceptance Criteria - sbp skill togglable --cwd

--json lists every skill with a deterministic state (on|off|missing_for_cwd|pinned_on|pinned_off), pinned_by, and a command_to_flip that, when executed, transitions the state as advertised. - Output is golden-locked and fixture-relative. Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- sbp skill togglable --cwd

--json lists every skill with a deterministic state (on|off|missing_for_cwd|pinned_on|pinned_off), pinned_by, and a command_to_flip that, when executed, transitions the state as advertised. - Output is golden-locked and fixture-relative. Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: Implemented sbp skill togglable and schema docs

title: sbp skill what-if — pure simulation of the effective set (zero disk touch)
description:
Files: .env-manager/runtime_manager/audit_report.py + .env-manager/runtime_manager/machines.py (re-root any repo/machine); scripts/sbp (skill what-if); tests/test_skill_overrides.py

AUDIENCE: an agent/operator deciding before they leap. Bar: 'if I enable overlay marketing in repo ../web on machine box-7, exactly which skills appear/shadow/survive?' answered as a diff, no symlink written.

WHAT: `sbp skill what-if --repo <reg-id|path> --overlay X --pin Y --opt-out Z --machine M --json` returns {effective, added, removed, shadowed_by_layer, pin_conflicts} as a PURE FUNCTION of inputs — route through the SAME resolver as the effective set with an injected in-memory model (no forked logic), writing zero files. Reuse registry/machines re-rooting to simulate any repo on any machine (read-only by construction).

DONE: what-if --overlay X for a fixture repo equals `overlay activate X --dry-run` effective set (byte-equal modulo host paths); what-if writes zero files (assert mtimes unchanged); an unknown machine returns a structured error, not a crash. TEST: tests/test_skill_overrides.py; python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on the precedence spine.

## Acceptance Criteria
- sbp skill what-if --overlay X for a fixture repo equals overlay activate X --dry-run effective set (byte-equal modulo host paths) and writes ZERO files (mtimes unchanged on the override file and link tree).
- An unknown machine returns a structured error, not a crash; the resolver is shared with the effective-set path (no forked logic).
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- sbp skill what-if --overlay X for a fixture repo equals overlay activate X --dry-run effective set (byte-equal modulo host paths) and writes ZERO files (mtimes unchanged on the override file and link tree).
- An unknown machine returns a structured error, not a crash; the resolver is shared with the effective-set path (no forked logic).
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: done

title: Route m/marketing + overlay verbs through the override layer; add per-repo overlay state
description:
Files: .env-manager/runtime_manager/policy_eval.py (active_overlays ~436 — accept cwd); scripts/sbp (m/marketing + overlay dispatch ~1059-1080); tests/test_per_repo_overlay.py (new), tests/test_mode_pack_overlays.py

AUDIENCE: operator wanting an overlay (hardening/marketing) ON in one repo, OFF elsewhere. Bar: per-repo overlay state composes with the global overlay file under one defined precedence and one `why`.

WHAT: make active_overlays() accept a cwd and union/subtract the repo file's overlays.enable/disable over the global ~/.skillbox-state/overlays state (repo-disable beats global-on; repo-enable beats global-off; env SKILLBOX_OVERLAYS stays top, ephemeral). Route `sbp m`/marketing and overlay on/off/toggle through this SAME override layer (write overlays.enable:[marketing] for durable, or SKILLBOX_OVERLAYS for ephemeral) — do NOT give marketing its own state store. A cwd-less active_overlays() call degrades to today's global-only behavior (back-compat).

DONE: global overlays empty + repo enable:[hardening] → hardening rules active at that cwd only; global hardening on + repo disable:[hardening] → inactive there, active elsewhere; `why` attributes the overlay decision to the repo-file layer. TEST: tests/test_per_repo_overlay.py + tests/test_mode_pack_overlays.py; python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on the spine and why.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Global overlays empty + repo overlays.enable:[hardening] -> hardening-gated rules active at that cwd only; global hardening on + repo overlays.disable:[hardening] -> inactive there but active elsewhere.
- sbp m/marketing and overlay on/off/toggle route through the override layer (no separate marketing store); a cwd-less active_overlays() degrades to today's global-only behavior; why attributes the overlay decision to the repo-file layer.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- Global overlays empty + repo overlays.enable:[hardening] -> hardening-gated rules active at that cwd only; global hardening on + repo overlays.disable:[hardening] -> inactive there but active elsewhere.
- sbp m/marketing and overlay on/off/toggle route through the override layer (no separate marketing store); a cwd-less active_overlays() degrades to today's global-only behavior; why attributes the overlay decision to the repo-file layer.
- Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: done

title: sbp skill lint / doctor validation of the override file (contradictions, dead entries, floor opt-out)
description:
Files: .env-manager/runtime_manager/validation.py + .env-manager/runtime_manager/structure_doctor.py (non-fatal collector like _LAST_SCOPE_RULE_ERRORS); scripts/sbp (skill lint, doctor); tests/test_override_doctor.py (new)

AUDIENCE: anyone hand-editing or machine-generating the override file (heal/promote do). Bar: contradictory or stale overrides are flagged before they surprise someone.

WHAT: static analysis of one override file against policy + the precedence spine → findings [{rule, severity, skill, explanation, suggested_fix}]: contradiction (pin_on X + pin_off X), no-op (opt_out_global of a non-global; pin_off of an already-absent skill), shadowed (a pin that never wins under a higher layer; info-severity, --verbose), dangling (pin to an unresolvable source; did-you-mean), opt_out of a FLOOR skill (hard fail). Use the non-fatal collector pattern (like _LAST_SCOPE_RULE_ERRORS) so one bad line doesn't nuke the report. Surface via `sbp skill lint` and fold into `sbp doctor`/recalibrate.

DONE: pin_on X + pin_off X → contradiction naming both lines; opt_out_global smart → hard floor error; pin to a deleted skill → dangling; clean file → zero findings. TEST: tests/test_override_doctor.py; python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on the schema reader and the error taxonomy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- pin_on X + pin_off X yields a contradiction finding naming both lines; opt_out_global of smart/sbp yields a hard floor error; a pin to a deleted skill yields a dangling/did-you-mean finding; a clean file yields zero findings.
- The non-fatal collector pattern is used (one bad line does not nuke the report). Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- pin_on X + pin_off X yields a contradiction finding naming both lines; opt_out_global of smart/sbp yields a hard floor error; a pin to a deleted skill yields a dangling/did-you-mean finding; a clean file yields zero findings.
- The non-fatal collector pattern is used (one bad line does not nuke the report). Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
close_reason: Implemented repo-local skill override lint surfaced via manage.py/sbp skill lint and sbp structure doctor; covers contradiction line reporting, dispatcher floor opt-out, dangling did-you-mean, clean file, parse-error nonfatal collector, normal source-root discovery, and command-registry search/explain. Verified: python3 -m unittest tests.test_override_doctor tests.test_skill_overrides tests.test_skill_visibility tests.test_cli_units tests.test_agent_ops_command_registry tests.test_agent_ops_search; python3 .env-manager/manage.py search 'skill lint' --format json; python3 .env-manager/manage.py explain runtime.skill_lint --format json; ./scripts/sbp skill lint --format json; python3 -m unittest discover -s tests (1727 tests OK, skipped=4).

title: sbp skill default --repos a,b | --category frontend — declarative cross-repo write (dry-run==apply, reversible)
description:
Files: .env-manager/runtime_manager/fleet_converge.py + .env-manager/runtime_manager/fleet_relink.py + .env-manager/runtime_manager/machines.py (registry/category re-root); scripts/sbp (skill default --repos/--category); .skillbox/skill-overrides.yaml (NEW, repo-committed); tests/test_fleet_converge.py

AUDIENCE: the operator managing >3 repos who today can only AUDIT cross-repo and must SSH-and-edit each box by hand. Bar: 'set X on for all frontend repos' is one declarative, portable, idempotent op.

WHAT: extend `sbp skill default` with cohort selectors — `--repos htma,htma-server` or `--category frontend` — resolving registry ids/buckets to current-machine paths (reuse the existing fleet_converge/relink resolver + machines.yaml re-rooting), then writing a .skillbox/skill-overrides.yaml pin/default into EACH resolved repo. --dry-run lists the exact files + diffs; apply writes the identical set; re-running is a no-op. High blast radius: gate on clean trees, require dry-run-first, never write to repo roots readable by in-container agents. Honest about atomicity: it's a saga with compensating undos across machines, not 2-phase commit — report any residue explicitly.

DONE: --category frontend --dry-run enumerates exactly the bucket members' override-file paths; apply writes them; re-run idempotent; an unknown id raises RegistryResolutionError with a did-you-mean. TEST: tests/test_fleet_*.py on fixture_fleet; python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. Depends on the default verb.

## Acceptance Criteria
- default --category frontend --dry-run enumerates exactly the bucket members' override-file paths; apply writes them and is idempotent on re-run; an unknown registry id raises RegistryResolutionError with a did-you-mean.

title: Define .skillbox/skill-overrides.yaml schema + git-root-anchored reader + validator + fail-safe parse
description:
Files: .skillbox/skill-overrides.yaml (NEW, repo-committed); .env-manager/runtime_manager/policy_eval.py (new _repo_override_policy + git-root walk ~1386); .env-manager/runtime_manager/errors.py (OVERRIDE_PARSE_ERROR); tests/test_skill_overrides.py (new), tests/fixture_fleet.py

AUDIENCE: operator (writes durable defaults, reviewed in git) + AI agents (read/write pins). Bar: a committed, machine-portable, reviewable file expresses 'in THIS repo, skill X is on/off' without touching external policy or host state.

WHAT: specify and implement the schema (version: 1; pin_on: [..]; pin_off: [..]; opt_out_global: [..]; overlays: {enable: [..], disable: [..]}; defaults: [..]; reason: "..."). Add a reader _repo_override_policy(cwd) that anchors at the GIT ROOT (reuse the _repo_root_for_skill_install / _git_repo_root_for_beads walk-up at policy_eval.py:1386 — NOT cwd, so a pin set from a subdir lands in the repo's file). Add a schema validator that REJECTS unknown keys (blocks smuggling future privileged fields). Malformed YAML must FAIL SAFE to base policy (never crash) and surface error code OVERRIDE_PARSE_ERROR.

WHY: this file is the chosen durable-defaults mechanism that must survive recalibrate; anchoring + validation + fail-safe are its trust foundations. CONSIDERATIONS: keep it a pure INPUT (read at eval), never a generated output; round-trip-preserving writes come in Phase 3.

DONE: reader returns a typed override policy from a fixture repo; unknown key → validation error naming the key; truncated/invalid YAML → base-policy result + OVERRIDE_PARSE_ERROR (no traceback); subdir invocation resolves to the repo-root file. TEST: new tests/test_skill_overrides.py via tests/fixture_fleet.py (extend it with .skillbox/ files); python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reader returns a typed override policy from a fixture repo; an unknown key yields a validation error naming the key.
- Malformed YAML fails safe to the base-policy result with code OVERRIDE_PARSE_ERROR and no traceback.
- A subdir invocation resolves to the git-root override file. Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests (tests/test_skill_overrides.py).

title: Journaled skill-state transactions — snapshot, undo, redo, checkout @time
description:
Files: .env-manager/runtime_manager/agent_snapshots.py (extend normalizer to skill-link set + override hash); .env-manager/runtime_manager/lifecycle.py (inverse plan); .skillbox-state/snapshots/skill_tx/; tests/

AUDIENCE: any agent that mutates skill visibility mid-session. Bar: every toggle is reversible by construction — `sbp skill-tx undo` restores the exact prior symlink+override state, redo re-applies, checkout materializes any past state.

WHAT: a journal under .skillbox-state/snapshots/skill_tx/ of {tx_id, parent_snapshot, result_snapshot, plan, inverse_plan, ts}. REUSE the existing agent_snapshots.py (deterministic redaction, stable-hash IDs, save/load_snapshot) — extend its normalizer to capture the effective skill-link set + the override-file hash (scope it to skill state ONLY, not unrelated repo churn). inverse_plan is computed from the SAME lifecycle plan builder (link↔unlink swap), so undo is provably correct given the proven dry-run==apply byte-equality. Journal lock for multi-pane (the send-later heartbeat pattern is precedent).

WHY this is the substrate: heal/promote/fleet-apply/recipes all assume 'mutate safely and reversibly' — with a tx log each collapses to 'build a plan, commit a tx'. DONE: property test — for any random op sequence over fixture_fleet, apply;undo returns to a snapshot whose stable-hash equals the pre-op snapshot; apply;undo;redo equals post-op; checkout <id> reaches a tree hashing to <id>. TEST: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests. DEFERRED — not on the critical path. Depends on the firewall and on/off.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A property test over fixture_fleet holds: for any random op sequence, apply;undo returns to a snapshot whose stable-hash equals the pre-op snapshot; apply;undo;redo equals post-op; checkout <id> reaches a tree hashing to <id>.
- The snapshot normalizer (reused from agent_snapshots.py) is scoped to skill-link state + override-file hash only. Verify: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.
acceptance_criteria:
